top_profane.text: Don't buy this unless you like to spend your time wondering why you ever spent so much money on such a worthless piece of crap. (Punny?) First off, I have two indoor cats. I purchased this litter box because I was tired of harassing my eleven year old to scoop the poop every single day. I looked at all the glowing reviews and figured this would be great!! Automatic scooper? Lasts up to two weeks for two cats? No smell? Hell, I'm IN! I purchased the box and three refills to the tune of $190. Thinking back, I want to smack myself. Really?! Who the fruck spends $190 for something cats poop in? I spend lots of time debating over whether I should spend $11 on my toilet paper or $16 for the good stuff. But cardboard, magic blue crystals, and a metal rake to shovel poo? Sold! Idiot. Day one and two were great. Kitties poo or pee in the box, three minutes later the rake neatly shovels it into a little cardboard cubby to be stored away for two whole weeks!! What an invention...why didn't I get this sooner? I'm happy, cats are happy, and my eleven year old is certainly overjoyed. Day four...I woke up the auto rake doing it's job...but it stunk. The odor of cat crap hung in the air all night and by morning, I was pretty sure I could taste the cat poop in my mouth. There was a cat poop fog permeating the entire upstairs. My kid came out of her room and complained about smelling cat crap all night. I went to take a look at the box...and saw that my cat had crapped near the edge of the box and the rake had scooped it up and drug it aaaallll the way down to the magic cubby, leaving a trail of smeared cat crap all over the side of the plastic. Also, neatly encased in three of the rake tines was compacted cat crap covered in blue litter crystals. Are you effing kidding me?! I had to take the box apart and clean the whole thing. I got four days out of the first litter tray, and two hours on a Saturday morning spent scraping compacted cat turds out of the magic rake, and wiping off a trail of cat waste smeared like Nutella down the side of the box. Fast forward to six days later and the litter box smells like the inside of my cats bowels. Really?!? What happened to the two week promise?!? I could smell the litter box from downstairs. Litter tray #2 into the trash can. A few nights later I am awoken by the sound of the rake. Try to go back to sleep. Hear one of the cats scratching in the litter box...rake. Scratch scratch scratch....wait three mins....rake. Scratch scratch scratch....wait three mins, rake. WTF?! I go take a look at the cats are playing in the litter box?! Hopping out and watching the rake level out the crystals...and then hopping back in the box. Wtf? Wtf? Wtf? I unplugged it. A couple of days later, I awake to find crap encased in the rake. Again. And the cardboard liner, which has only been in place for five days, has leaked through to my carpet. After spending three hours cleaning the litter box and scrubbing my carpet, I plugged it back in and used the last of my $20 litter trays. Next day? Cat turd in the rake again. I carried it downstairs and tossed it in the trash. I'm back to nagging my eleven year old to scoop the clumping cat waste and way happier with my $30 litter box and $12 box of litter that lasts a month. Don't waste your money. Or your time! This product sucks!!!
top_profane.title: Poopy Rake

top_profane.text:
TLDR was I received it on November 27, 2022 and it had crapped out by January 2023. If you want the details keep reading.

In January I started getting "Over torque fault" error messages which resulted in blinking yellow and red lights and sometimes left the globe in the wrong position. The Litter Robot 3 comes with no actual instruction book so I had no means of figuring out what the light codes meant. (I still don't.) I began searching online and found no one with my exact problems, but I found many people with many other problems usually related to the sensors. The best I could find was that maybe my issue was related to the weight of the litter. I stopped adding litter and even when it was down to almost no litter the faults continued so I switched to "light weight" litter and even with that the faults continued. During this period I took it apart several times to clean it, check for obstructions, and even tried wiping down the track with furniture polish per the internet. None of this worked. Last week the light changed to solid blue and solid yellow and at that point I contacted Whisker. I emailed them and included everything that I put above and the response was this:

Hi Ed

Based on your comment that the unit only cycles at certain times, or the unit has a blue and yellow indicator light lit, your unit is in 8-hour sleep mode. During the 8-hour sleep mode, your unit will not cycle. The unit will show the blue and yellow LED during the sleep mode.
Customer Service Code: 3034
To reset or quit sleep mode, press and hold the Cycle button until the yellow LED flashes on then off.

So essentially, they didn't even read the entire issue, just the last line. I replied to that effect and they apologized and immediately offered to send me a new base and a new power supply. According to Whisker (or at least the first person I had contact with) you shouldn't fill the litter robot to the fill line he recommended never filling it to within a half inch of the fill line. My issue is this: I just wanted some reassurance that this isn't a widespread issue and that my litter robot was just a fluke. The idea that we went directly to complete replacement without even trying to trouble shoot makes me feel like this isn't a fluke and gives me the idea that I am at least fairly likely to get another bad copy. Also, if the tolerances on this machine are so narrow that filling it once to the line (when I got it) and refilling it maybe 3 times in the month I used it can ruin the whole machine then how can I seriously expect a replacement to actually last any significant period of time? And how can they ship these things with a "fill line" and no instructions and expect you to know that if you actually use that fill line you'll destroy your machine??

In the end I was told that I had a 90 day window for a refund. I replied that I would be willing to give the replacement a try, but I want my 90 day window started over. I feel this a perfectly reasonable request given the circumstances however this is the response I got today for that:

Hello Ed,

Thank you for your reply.

Before we send any replacement parts, I checked into your request for the additional 90 days. Unfortunately, the most we can extend it is 30 days from February 25, 2023, giving you until March 27, 2023, to return for a refund if you so wish to do. This will give you plenty of time to decide if you wish to keep the unit or return it for a refund. If you move forward with the replacement, we recommend retaining the box, just in case, as we cannot ship boxes out.

Please let me know if you wish to proceed with the replacement base and power supply. If so, I will place a rush on that replacement order so that it will ship from our warehouse within 72 business hours.

Kind regards,

Actually, to verify, they don't "recommend" anything... the litter robot 3 came with basically no documentation. In requesting the 90 day extension I was just looking to see if they felt that this is a fluke thing and the 90 days is no big deal because the problem will have been solved. What this tells me is that they know the product has issues and they won't stand behind it. I had told them that I no longer have the box and they seem to think that this now becomes my problem. I replied that I will be going for the refund. Nothing they have done gives me any confidence that they can (or want to) fix the issue. The only idea I can think of is that to send it back I'm going to have to cut the thing up to fit it into a decent sized box.

In summary as I put in the title this is a genius level idea. When it works - it's amazing. But the company that makes it seems to be doubling down on bad ideas as well. If you are going to charge that much include some instructions! If the "fill line" is actually an "over fill line" then include something that warns your customers. If this is actually a "small business" as Amazon states then it is not that they "can't" give me that 90 day extension it is that they "won't" give me that extension. (30 days isn't enough, the first one made it 30 days.)

I haven't heard back from Whisker on how I am supposed to return this thing or if they seriously expect me to pay for the shipping, but I'll update as this progresses.

Update 2/8/23: I got a message from Whisker telling me that they will send me a return form and that it is going to cost me $60 out of my refund. This message came through Amazon and I have no means of replying to it. I emailed back the other person I had been communicating with and told them about the $60 and asked about how I am supposed to return this. I had an Amazon return before where I was able to drop off an item with the return authorization and they packaged it up and took care of it for me. Here was the reply I received on 2/7/23:

Hello Ed,

Thank you for your reply.

I do apologize for the inconvenience that this has caused you. I have spoken with one of our Amazon team members, and they will reach out to you soon. Please be sure to respond to them.

Kind regards,

She certainly wants me to stop communicating with her and I assure you the feeling is mutual. No answers. "Please be sure to respond to them." You can't. They know this. So I replied again and we're now at the point that if I email them I get no response until the next day. I again asked about the drop off point and about the $60. I let them know that I want this resolved before I do this. I don't want to ship that back and two weeks later find out that they stole $60 from me out of my refund. (I strongly suspect that somehow/someway I'm not getting all of my money back.) So today I get this response:

Hello Ed,

Please see the Amazon reply from Ms. *****. She confirmed that a label was sent to you via Amazon messages and that the shipping fee was being waived. The label is through FedEx. For further information regarding your return, please send those to the Amazon message corresponding with you. They will be able to provide more information than I can as they handle all Amazon orders and returns.

Take care, and have a wonderful rest of your day!

I put ***** in place of her name. I've intentionally left all the names except mine out of this. In any case I copy/pasted the message I had from Amazon with the name "Whisker" on it. I had no idea who was sending it to me and it clearly stated I was going to be charged $60. There was no answer to my question about the drop point. I told the other lady that it said I would be charged and it answered none of my questions and yet again that I can't reply. A couple hours later I got another Amazon message:
Hello Ed,

"Previous Lady" has been working with you and we need you to go through the return process here in Amazon as this is where you ordered from.

We are waiving the return fee but you do need to secure a box on your own as we do not provide packing materials.

We do not provide any shipping supplies or packaging services. For supplies, we recommend trying a shipping goods store (U-Haul or Rental facilities), general store (Home Depot, Lowe's, etc), or local grocery store. Most big box-type stores have large shipping boxes that cost under $10. You will also want to use other mailing supplies such as bubble wrap to ensure a safe ride here.

Original packing is recommended; however, all we ask is that it is boxed securely to allow a safe ride to our facility. If using the original packaging, please remove the old shipping label. This will prevent the package from being re-delivered to you by mistake.

**Shipping Box Dimensions**
Litter-Robot 3 box weighs 32 pounds and its dimensions are 32" x 24" x 24". You can use boxes that are slightly larger (an inch or two). It does not have to be that exact size. U-Haul and Rental facilities will usually sell boxes of this size. Home Depot sells wardrobe boxes that are this size as well. You can view it here .

So once again I bought their product in good faith and it ends up on me to figure out how to get it back. I am going to incur expense in order to return their faulty product. They also included in their instructions that I have to clean it and if they are not satisfied with how well I clean it that they plan to steal $75 out of my return. I will take pictures before I box it up and I will keep track of my expenses and invoice them for it.

If you buy a litter robot this is what you can look forward to dealing with. Keep in mind that if they had just agreed to the 90 days on the new parts we wouldn't be going through this. Also, theoretically, I could order a new one right now. When it comes in I could use the box/packing to send the old one back and I'd have my 90 days. I won't do that.

This thing was a phenomenal idea and for the 40ish days that it worked I loved it. Evidently 3 generations in they've only managed a little over a month of doing the job it is supposedly designed to do. Maybe all the issues are ironed out in the LR4... maybe not. They'll never get another penny of my money. I'm off to buy a litter box and I'll go back to scooping it. Know what else?? I'm extremely confident that 40 days from now that litter box will still be functioning as it is designed to. As for Whisker - They have had 139 Better Business Bureau complaints in the last 3 years and 119 of those were in the last 12 months. It doesn't sound like they have fixed anything.

I'll update when something new happens.

Update: Shipped back 2/21/23. I was not charged for shipping by fedex. I included an invoice for the $56.12 that the box/packing materials/tape cost me along with a copy of the receipt for those materials. Now I get to wait to see how much they short change me and what (if any) excuse they give for doing it. Sadly, they have given no indication of integrity. I'll be highly shocked if they do the right thing and make me whole. I will update when something new happens again.

03/12/23 - This is the third time I've tried to update after shipping the LR3 back and Amazon hasn't put up the other two. Just checking to see if I'm being completely censored or just not allowed to criticize Whisker any more.

03/17/23 - Almost 3 weeks have gone by since Whisker received the package with my LR3. I have had no communication from them and have not received a refund of any sort. Customer service at it's finest. This is a perfect example of what you can expect if you try to deal with this company.

3/22/23 (Final update - probably) - Ok, today... 23 days after Whisker received my package I had enough waiting and I contacted them again. I restated that I had sent them back the LR3 on 2/22 and that they had received it on 2/27 and now over 3 weeks later I had received NO communication from them and NO refund. I further stated that if I did not receive my refund that I would sue and that I don't care if I spend more than what I'll get I will not allow them to steal that money from me. I sent that message at 3:40PM. Amazingly.... 8 minutes later I got a reply:

Hi there,
We do apologize for the delay and very much appreciate your patience. We have received your unit and processed a refund. You can expect to see the money refunded to your account within 5-7 business days.
Please let us know if you have any additional questions.
Best regards,
Whisker

I am stunned with disbelief.... I guess all I had to do was beg a little. They simply were not capable of just doing what was right, honoring their own "guarantee", or just try to make right an obvious wrong. No, they couldn't. I had to ask for it.

Roughly two hours after that I received an email from Amazon telling me that my refund had been processed and will be available in a couple days. I honestly have no doubts in Amazon. So at this point I do believe that I will get back the original $749.68 that I spent to buy it in the first place. There was no mention of the $56.12 that the box/packing materials cost me to send their trash back to them. So, in addition to the frustration of the last few months I'm also $56.12 poorer for having been stupid enough to purchase their product.

A little additional news: If you check out Whisker's Better Business Bureau listing it is now listed as "being updated" and you can't see any of the complaints or numbers that were there before. The last time a looked a few weeks ago they had 139 complaints in the last 3 years and 119 of them were in the last 12 months as I mentioned earlier in the review.

In summary - the one consistent factor for this company/product is crap. It's a product that is supposed to deal with crap. It works like crap. It's a crap product made by a crap company. Said company has crap tech support and crap customer service. I can't suggest it, you're better off just dealing with your cat's crap.

-Ed
top_profane.title: Genius level idea - horrible execution

top_profane.text:
I actually just want to share my experience with this product, specifically the large container. I will admit, I never knew Amazon sold this or I would have paid a lot less. I am going to purchase 1 more right now from Amazon for the office in our house where we sometimes sit and have snacks and throw the non recyclable food containers in the garbage. Yes, the office trash can attract flies too, we unfortunately recently found out.

Our house was a new construction and we are fairly clean in the kitchen. No dishes in the sink. They go right to the dishwasher and counter tops stayed cleaned after each use. So how in the world did we get such an ant infestation?

Well, it doesn't matter because it happened. They were sugar ants and they loved our kitchen garbage can. Did it have a lid? Sure! Was it airtight? Heck no!!! The ants were too fat and happy. So I searched all over for an airtight garbage can and couldn't find one. Using the word "airtight" led me to this delightful item that I bought at a local store. Any tall kitchen garbage bags fits it perfectly. When I put a new bag in, I first open up the bag, put it into the container, I pull the opening to the bag over the rim of the container (this does not affect the airtight seal in any way) and then I make sure the bag is opened up to the bottom of the bag so garbage drops right down.

It works! We have had the 1st one (still used as a garbage can) for at least 9 years now. The ants hated us. HeeHee. This thing works like a champ at keeping insect pests out. Extremely durable, too. We must open and close that thing 2 dozen times per day for 9 years and the gasket is still in great shape.

This has worked out so great for us, when our kitchen recycling (not composting) bin started getting flies in them, I got another one of these air tight containers. Then we learned of the county recycling program that took anything the residential program didn't (and it was a significant amount of food containers and any other household recyclable material), we got yet another.

How airtight? You don't smell any orders until that lid opens. Our garbage is normally not noticeably stinky, but one onion season, the discarded onion skins in the garbage got a rotted smell overnight. You had no clue that had happened till the first time you flip open the lid in the morning and were bowled over by the stink. I keep a removable air freshner in there now (removable so I can easily clean the container), but that's just to deal with when we open the lid that has a stinky bag of garbage.

To clean the lid, just use your favorite bleach based spray cleaner. Put the dirty, sometimes moldy lid in your kitchen sink and spray one side generously. Let sit for 15 minutes. If you still see things, spray again. Wipe with cloth when done, no water. Flip it over and do the same to the other side. Too grossed out to put it in the kitchen sink (remember, you are using a bleach based cleaner which kills 99.9% of the germs and does just peachy on the mold too), put it in your bath tub or any large utility sink instead .

You can clean and sanitize the inside of this container in the same way (I've even used a Lysol spray that claims to kill 99.9% of household germs, and mold in this case). I might have to spray it with the spray several times, it gets it all nice and clean and I just wipe up. I prefer the Lysol spray for the container because I don't have to move the garbage or recycle containers to protect our wood floors from bleach.

Someone have a cold and you don't want the snap close opener to transmit illnesses? Keep bleach-based wipes conveniently near the container and just keep wiping it down. So easy.

In total, these are extremely good for the price. No, I haven't any pets (or ants for that matter :-) ). I never thought I would be okay with this as a kitchen garbage can because I like foot pedals. I've learned that despite the snap close opener that keeps the tight seal on the container when snapped closed, you can use the back of your hand to easily flip it open and easily snap it close with your knuckles. So no touching with your fingers.

From all the garbage cans I've ever owned, I like this the best and it was never meant to be a garbage can. You can use the wheels if you want. We don't. You have to snap the wheels in, so it is an option to not use them.

Pros: No insect pests, no linger orders in the kitchen, incredibly easy to keep clean even with corrosive products such as bleach (bleach on chrome and metal don't quite get along), no discoloring ever (not even yellowing from bleach based cleaners) and after 9 years of owning the first one that is in constant use, no signs of wear.

Cons: I'm trying to think of one. Oh, I have 3, but they are no big deal. Sometimes the trash bag has to be reseated over the rim of the container. The 2nd one is when you go to clean the lid, it is a little bit difficult to pull off (for cleaning) and to put back on. In both cases, try one side of the lid first, then the other. The 3rd con is that despite the lid being a little difficult to pull off to clean, once in a blue moon, it inexplicably comes off when you lift the lid. The fact it does come off so I can get it squeaky clean is a "Pro" to me, so I tolerate this little occasional inconvenience.

In the category of garbage cans this is the most functional and easy to maintain one I've ever had.

So if it can do all this for 9 years as a garbage can and you need it for its intended use for pet food, rest assured you'll love it. Just when you clean it, you will have to rinse off the all the cleaning products well to keep chemicals away from your pet(s)'s food. I recommend using a tall kitchen trash bag to line your pet food container as an extra protective layer.

That's it. I hope your experience has been as good as ours. I hope this helps!
top_profane.title: Got Ants? Bugs in your kitchen got you going crazy?

top_profane.text:
I bought the XXL version and followed the instructions that came with the package: open it and let it rest for a couple of days BEFORE using it so it has time to expand to its advertised size... even though I felt tempted to use it before that time... I did however take the cover off and let the bitch test it w/o the foam as if it were just a blanket and she loved it, also ended up using only the Comy Canvas size for a reason explained below in the "Cons" (check it out). I bought this bed to use it inside a metal wire dog crate that had the same dimensions and it fit just perfectly. The bitch did pee once on the bed "because", the foam didn't get wet so the waterproof seems to work just fine. The cover comes with washing instructions so I followed them and it was just fine, it didn't come apart in the washing machine or the drying machine.

So far I had it for only 9 days but here is what I have to say about it:

Pros:
The dimensions was exactly as stated in the product description 48"x30"x3".
The Deluxe polyester AKA the "softer and warmer" side feels very soft and the bitch loved it.
The fabric of the cover feels very high quality for the price.
There is a small covering at the end of the zipper line to cover the zipper head underneath so the dog cant see it and prevents the dog from pulling it, chewing it, opening the zipper, etc.
The foam inside the cover is protected with a plastic cover to water proof it, it has its own zipper.
Can easily rip off the washing instructions w/o damaging the cover.
The foam can be easily replaced by whatever you want if one day you desire to.
The foam is soft but sturdy enough for a 46lbs dog so it does not completely sink when on top, the experience might be different with a heavier dog but worst case scenario you can just get a foam that suits your particular needs.
Can be washed on a washing machine.
Can be dried on a drying machine.
My bitch really likes to sleep on it.

Cons:
The zipper head is a bit difficult to push into the zipper cover at the end of the line, I recommend using something like an ice-cream wooden or plastic stick to push it in a bit until the zipper head thread to pull it out is at least flush with the cover so the dog cant spot it and start pulling on it. I push the threat a little bit further and just use a tweezer to pull the thread out a bit so I can pull it.
The Deluxe polyester side although feeling great may not be good for dogs that like to chew or pull it, my bitch began to pull it a minute after she was laying on top of it so I had to reverse her bed and have only allowed her to use it on the Comy Canvas "Cool" side since then. I don't know if the material is hazardous or if the strands are long enough to pose a threat to a dogs intestines but I decided not to put that to the test, so If you decide to use the warmer side make sure you pay attention to your dog as soon as he begins to use it until at least from 30min to 1hr, and from time to time check on your dog.
The plastic cover for the foam has a strong new-plastic smell, I found it a bit intrusive but my bitch could care less.

Overall I like it and my bitch seems to like it too, so I 100% recommend it to anyone.
top_profane.title: Bitch immediately accepted it as her new bed

top_profane.text:
UPDATED after a couple more weeks of trying to make it work- I still pretty much the same way about the product, but I wanted to add a little additional information.
1. Daily cleaning and maintenance helps a ton.
2. Keeping a large trash bin nearby to help reduce the mess helps. I just pull the bottom plastic bin out and throw the whole thing in an outdoor trash can. I don't worry about putting the lid on
3. This product has definitely helped with the ammonia like smell that we had with our traditional box.
4. Overall I think the product is just as much work and probably just as messy as the traditional litter box (if not more), however it has helped with the smell.
5. I worry about how long it will last, as it seems to struggle when raking the litter (described below in original post).

6. The manufacturer has reached out to us based on my 1 star review, and has offered to make things right - which these days is pretty rare.

I will update this if my opinion changes. I am truly trying to make this POS work. My wife bought it for me, so that I would have an easier time taking care of the litter while she is pregnant. I really do appreciate her trying to help me out and I am trying to make this thing work. My thoughts after two weeks with the mega-

1. Needs to be dumped every day (have two 10 year old 10lb cats

2. The plastic trays don't fit right (get ready to get up close and dirty trying to get them in the right spot)

3. Loud and the motor and gears sound like they are ready to die already

4. The ramp thing is a joke. Comes off every time you try to empty the crap basket

5. Keep a broom and vacuum nearby. Kitty litter goes everywhere when attempting to change the crap basket and/or when cats use it. Just as messy if not more so than traditional.

6. Be prepared to constantly manually rake the litter and clean the "auto" rake. The piss and crap gets caked up even with the high dollar litter. So far this process is more work than the old traditional

Again, I appreciate my wife's intentions but his thing is an overpriced complete waste of money. How it ever got to production is beyond me. I guess enough rich pet owners aimlessly buy this kind of crap. All that said- I am going to keep trying for another two weeks. After that if it doesn't work then it will be unplugged and become a $100 traditional litter box or possibly used for target practice (depending on how pissed off I am with it at the moment). Wish me luck......
top_profane.title: Trying to make it work.......listen to the all the poor reviews!!!!!

top_profane.text:
TL:DR - 6 square feet of old cat piss in carpet neutralized, can have guests over that will also come back!

Gather around, children, as I tell you a tale of a woman who was lifted out of a pit of madness and despair...
I took over the lease for a friend-of-friend's(FF) apartment after they moved out of state for work. This FF had two cats and one nasty litterbox in the tiny bathroom, and while I did check the place out while they still lived in the unit, I thought the bathroom was just the issue. I admit, I screwed up royally when I told management that "no, I don't want cleaning to happen, I'll handle it," as I firstly thought I was taking the unit as-is in the transfer so to keep the rent low, and secondly didn't want to hurt this FF's deposit as they did me a solid favor in transferring their lease. Besides, I told the FF to not worry about leaving items behind or a mess, I'd happily take care of it, and also knew even if the professional cleaning happened I'd STILL do my own deep clean. I really wish I let management just replace the carpets before I ignored his warnings/questionings and signed the waver.

While the bathroom was the deathzone with litter literally cemented to the 1in hex tile floor, upon opening the front door for the first time since the FF moved out, it was a boot made of cat piss to the face. I guess the day we visited they had every window and door open and fans going cause it was not that bad outside of the bathroom. Rented a caret cleaner the following day and got the large bottle of pet specific formula. Did a double pass over all carpets, scrubbed everything down, mopped the hell out of that bathroom and walls with vinegar, baking soda and solvents, left fans going and windows open for 36 hours. Returned with the first car load of stuff to be harshly booted to the face once again, all that cleaning did NOTHING! Not a dent.

Fortunately I was straddling both leases for the month and didn't need to sleep in the new one yet, so I decided to do research before going to the management on hands and knees pleading to help me. Read about a 4 step process to neutralize cat stains which involved baking soda, followed by vinegar, then after that was cleaned up you did a short treatment of peroxide mixed with dish soap, then within 24 hours of that drying was the most important step: a cat urine neutralizer. Looked up what that really was, turns out it's enzymes and bacteria that literally make dinner out of the crystals in cat pee that get sticky and smell forever. Think of it like a smelly bowl of sugar and ants. You can wet sugar, it will dissolve, maybe you can soak up some of the sugar water, but when its dry you have that sticky mess crystalizing once again. But the neutralizer works like a bunch of sugar loving ants which get in there, eat up everything and then leave!

In the world of pet neutralizers, Nature's Miracle was what everyone swore by. So locally I picked up the only cat specific product I could find by them (a can of carpet foam) and decided to try the 4 step method in the bathroom as it remained the worst offender, using the foam even on the walls cause why the hell not. After some time of doing the steps and waiting in between I could not longer tell if it was actually working or my nose went into a coma, because it seemed as if the bathroom was now the best smelling room and I puttered about. I decided to call it a day and closed up EVERYTHING. I closed all the windows I had left open for 96 hours now, closed every door inside the unit from rooms to cabinets, turned off all the fans, and just left the place for 24 hours.

I had reasoning behind the closing everything off. I wanted to make sure that when I came back I could identify which rooms had the offense. I didn't want any "oh, maybe it's better cause I had the windows open... Oh, maybe this room smells cause of the other room..." The next day my fiance came to help me with the next round of item moving, and I could tell he was skeptical of my saying "I think the bathroom was smelling better..." on our way there, and that's fine, I was too. While I waited with the car in the ally and started unloading it, he went in with the first box of stuff and the keys. He took a long time to come back, but when he did:
"I don't know what sorcery was in that can, but the bathroom IS the best smelling room in the apartment. AND YOU HAD ALL THE WINDOWS AND DOORS CLOSED?!!"
Bingo. That was the exact reaction I was aiming for when I closed everything off. We continued to keep the doors closed after that so to pinpoint what was now the worst locations and that night I researched more and order this product specifically.

Once it came in, I started the nose to carpet hunt, doing a 3x3 foot section at a time before my nose would shut down, and sure enough I found two large areas of carpet with consolations of cat pee, both being abut 1x3 feet in area outside the bedroom door and from there going into the living room. Instructions said to just soak the carpet so it got to the underflooring and leave it. I went one step further and even walked on the carpet to sponge it around and deep in there, put markers around the edges of what I treated so no one else would end up with wet socks and it took about 48 hours to properly dry up. And guess what? 3 years of old cat piss. Gone. Replaced by the scent of the cleaner, which not entirely pleasant, is far more acceptable than piss. I only used about half the bottle and it saved me having to pay to have carpets ripped out.

I should be completely transparent here. While yes, it did make that big of a difference and is worth every penny and then some, I would buy it again in a heart beat, I either have yet to find all the spots, or on some of the more particularly hot days we had there was a flare up of the cat piss from deep in the carpet I didn't reach, but seriously, this stuff was a miracle. I have tried sniffing the same spots where the pee was to still smell only old carpet. It's been about a month now since I treated, I still smell the cleaner in part upon entering the apartment after being gone a day, sometimes the smell of cats wafts by, but that's okay compared to what I started with, and it goes away with open windows or air freshener.
top_profane.title: Sorcery in a bottle!  Praise God for products that actually work as they say!

top_profane.text:
If you have a multi-cat household, you really need Feliway! The problem is, it's VERY expensive at the pet stores and never seems to last the full 30 days that the package promises. I have a multi-cat household, and trust me, when the Feliway diffuser is empty, I know it before I even see that the diffuser is empty. The cats start acting up and nit-picking at each other.
I started using Feliway a probably 4-5 years ago, when I was having occasional pissing issues with my cats. We used to take our shoes off and set them by the door in this shoe tray so that mud or water wouldn't be tracked into the house. One of my cats was obsessed with sitting on our shoes, like he was some dragon in his lair of shoes. He would even push the shoes over to make a perfect shoe nest for himself, and drag shoes over to boost his lair. I started noticing the strong smell of piss, and the litter boxes were clean. I picked up a shoe, and saw they were in a puddle of old piss. The smell was indescribable. It was the strongest, stinkiest smell of rotting urine, it was gag inducing. We had to throw out half of the shoes, some were able to be saved with Nature's Miracle. And the cleanup was horrible. After the horrifying shoe pissing incident, I decided to buy some Feliway on Amazon. The prices were a fraction of the cost of what it sells for in the pet stores. The night I plugged in the two diffusers (one on each floor), the strangest thing happened. The house was quiet. Nighttime is usually when they roam around causing trouble with each other. The house was eerily quiet. I figured that it had to be coincidence. However, they continued to be much quieter and get along much better.
We tried our best not to keep putting shoes in that corner, but they would end up piling up after a few weeks. However, the shoe pissing stopped. After a while, I decided that the habit had been beaten, and stopped buying feliway for a month or two. Guess what? Shoes were pissed on!

So, now I always keep a diffuser going. I really should have a second going too, but even with the low prices of the refills on Amazon, it does add up. I do put it on my wish list for holidays and birthdays to help offset the cost. I notice a big difference when I have a second diffuser going upstairs. I've read that using a vacation timer set to go on and off throughout the day can "stretch" how long a bottle lasts. This is something I really need to try.

But yes, this stuff really is worth the hype!
top_profane.title: This stuff really works, even for cats who piss on shoes! A must have for multi-cat households!

top_profane.text:
After reading both the glowing and the scathing reviews of the CatGenie, I was hesitant to buy this product. Did it work perfectly as some people stated, or was it a $300 piece of cat crap as the haters suggested?

Since the idea of nasty litter box smell kept me from adopting a kitty for quite some time, I decided to go for it 10 months ago -- both adopting a lovely cat from the local shelter and making the $300 investment in the CatGenie. The kitty arrived first, so I had to use a "regular" litter box for about a week before the CatGenie arrived. This made me extra nervous, since our poor cat would have to use three different types of litter boxes in a matter of days. Some of the reviews (as well as the literature on the CatGenie website) stated that some cats have a hard time adapting to the CatGenie, so would our new kitty freak out and crap all over our house when the flushing cat box was introduced?

The CatGenie arrived and we pulled everything out of the box. We had it set up in about 15 minutes--it was actually quite easy to do. We filled the basin with Cat Genie granules and installed the sanitizing pack and then gave it a try. The first cycle ran and we stared at it like we were seeing television for the first time. It spins! The arm rakes the granules! Here comes the water! It dries the litter with hot air! So far, so good. As the literature suggested, we kept the "regular" litter box set up next to the CatGenie, but our little kitty was too smart for that. From the moment we set up the CatGenie, she wanted nothing to do with her old box. She immediately jumped in the CatGenie and scratched around, and by the end of the evening she had put it to use. Now, would the thing actually "scoop" that way it was supposed to or would it cause the smell of cooked cat crap to permeate the air of my home as some reviewers stated?

The answer is...sort of. Our kitty was pretty small when we got her, so the scooping wasn't quite as realiable as I would have hoped. I still had a scoop for the old litter box, so, after turning on the CatGenie's cleaning cycle, I simply scooped the solids and deposited them into the hopper instead of trusting the scooping arm to do it. Yes, I really gave this thing five stars and yet I feel that the scooping mechanism isn't quite perfect. But my biggest issues with a conventional litter box are the stench and the time/hassle/expense/nastiness of hauling pounds of ammonia-smelling, disgusting litter out of my house on a regular basis. Scooping cat crap for five seconds a day without having to bag it and haul it outside still makes me LOVE my CatGenie. And for the record, now that our cat is adult-sized, my boyfriend will often just turn the CatGenie on and let it scoop--and he says that it works fine. I still don't trust it so I still use the manual scoop. The idea of stinking up the house with eau de baked cat crap makes me not want to chance it.

Okay, here's a quick breakdown (if you're still with me):

1. This thing is AWESOME. It works (almost) exactly the way the literature states it does.
2. It works even with low water pressure, despite what some of the reviews state. We were living in a high-rise LEED Gold building (it was "exciting" to spend 10 minutes rinsing the shampoo from my hair every time I took a shower) when we got the CatGenie and we simply went to the hardware store and bought a y-connector with shut-off valves for about $10 so we could turn off the water to the washing machine while running the CatGenie. Since we only run the litter box once a day or so, it wasn't any big deal to spend a few extra seconds to turn the valves on and off with each use. Once we bought our house, we didn't need the y-connector with valves anymore. Our laundry room is on the third floor of our house but we still have no issues with "low pressure" at all. I even got brave one day and did a load of laundry while the CatGenie was running and neither machine got mad. Yay!
3. The scoop may or may not work perfectly, but I still think this thing is awesome. Just keep a scoop next to the CatGenie and take five seconds to scoop the solids into the hopper every time you run the machine.
4. Obviously, we don't use the cat activation setting. We prefer to run it once or twice a day ourselves. Easy!
5. There is virtually no cat smell in our home, even when we had our kitty in our 800 square-foot condo. This thing seriously works!
6. I am finding that the sanitizing cartridges last a lot longer than what the manufacturer states. In ten months with daily use, I'm still only on my second sanitizing cartridge.
7. Buy an extra box of granules when you buy the CatGenie. I found that I need to add a few granules about every month or so. But that being said, I still have 3/4 of a box left from the third box I have purchased since I got the CatGenie ten months ago.
8. My cat LOVES the CatGenie. As I mentioned, she started using it immediately, literally stepping over her "regular" litter box to get to it. And she also loves watching it run. It's like cat television!
9. Never lugging 30-pound bags of litter into the house again!
10. Never hauling urine-soaked, cat crap-filled litter out of the house ever again!

I heart the CatGenie!
top_profane.title: Miracle in a (cat) box!

top_profane.text:
I, (like many who negatively reviewed this DVD) had hoped this would have a bit more talking training, but there's only 8 sections, all very dull (one scared my birds... the Perch With a View had a raccoon in 1 scene which are natural predators of birds... how stupid can you get? Not a way to teach training... unless its how to "run & hide & not come out for any reason!!!" What a way to foster a trusting bond in their new foster home! And these folks are supposed to KNOW about bird behavior?) But beyond "I love you" & "Hello" which are the ONLY phrases taught on this entire DVD, my birds coulda cared less about seeing babies being fed in the nursery, or the big Macaws sitting there doing nuthin... which is largely what is on this DVD... in fact I do believe other than the brief & overly repetitive "step-up" chapter, that about covers whats on this disappointing DVD. I won't order anymore, cuz I do not like when a manufacturer over appraises their new DVD release to look like something spectacular... it was anything but. Save your money & order something from GoodBird Inc... cuz I swear I found better material downloading a few youtube vids of other birds talking & looped'em together in Mover Maker to make one long (and PRODUCTIVE EDUCATIONAL AND FUN) video for my birds. I mean DVD's can hold a ton of space... where's a few more phrases? They weren't even done according to how behaviorists say to teach birds. The "Flashcards" were nothing but the words on a box that popped up annoyingly every time the phrase was repeated. I'm glad I didn't order the others. I usually do not have to give bad reviews, & can almost always find SOMETHING good about any DVD, however small to mention... but this was crap from beginning to end! Nuthin that you or your bird is missing here. My birds already know how to eat & preen each other, & they are already saying full sentences, so any talking training DVD NEEDS to qualify the content w/a level like "for very beginning birds" or "if your bird cannot even say hello yet" but I made an audio recording of several phrases & funny cute things that my birds picked up a LOT quicker than this. My birds already know about 50 phrases, so this was a waste of time. They shoulda been HONEST & said "the only phrases on here for talking (2 out of the 8 sections) have anything involved w/talking on them, & include only 2 easy phrases "Hello" & "I Love You" & have lots of potentially scary things to smaller birds, so is really only appropriate for really young large parrots like harlequin macaws, or else your birds may be bored or scared... or both! Order with caution!" Just say it like it is Pet Media: We just took a few minutes worth of crappy video in our bird shop & thought we could make a ton of money duping pet owners & make it look actually educational when we're really having no clue what we're doing! We didn't even stop to think that huge harlequin macaws or footage of raccoons would scare your poor birdies, & we don't really care either... we are only in it for the money. Well Pet Media, I wan't my dang money back! (But I have to give a Kudos to the used book store on Amazon I purchased this from, at least the shipping was fast & only took 3 days, so I can at least mention that!) Next time have another 20 or so sections, at least 10-15 diff phrases, a range of birds & commands they can learn, some music (did you forget birdies LOVE music? It seems to help them learn too when words & phrases are put to a beat). Also since its possible for birds to actually learn the context & meaning of words, try actually having some relevant scenes for them to associate with the words like at least the "step-up" section had scenes of a bird actually stepping up. Also the lame "taming your bird" & "advanced taming" sections coulda given some tips & talked the owner thru how to accomplish this perfectly calm sweet bird being handled & petted, so that we could even get our young scared untame birds into that scenario when they won't even come to us, or bite the moment they see your hand or finger... so some tips on how to get my bird on his back laying still & calm woulda friggin helped ya know! The other sections of "preening" & feeding time" were nothing but video of birds preening ea other & being fed... I mean seriously? If we are supposed to play this for our birds while they're away, don't ya think you shoulda looped all the "for birds to watch" sections to together, cuz I had to manually choose ea section which lasted only a few brief mins... so the last I checked my birds did NOT know how to use a remote AND what the heck are the supposed to do for the other 7.5 hrs of the day once those sections are over? And "A Perch w/A View" had nuthin more than VERY boring video of plants... and more plants... some running water w/a raccoon (which scared my birds), a cockatiel sitting on a stick looking quite pissed off, & that's it! Tell me how that is supposed to do ANYTHING to help my birds? After that 5 mins are up, what then? After they've seen a angry cockatiel & a scary raccoon whose a bird's natural predator in the wild, how is this educational? Where does the "Adventure" part come in... cuz 5 minutes of "Hello" & seeing baby birds being fed & vomitting really seemed soooooo adventurous! WTH, did I miss something?... maybe some hidden 1 hr section w/actual content on it? DO NOT BUY THIS VIDEO CUZ IT BEYOND STINKS!!! INSTEAD SAVE YOUR MONEY, DOWNLOAD YOUTUBE VIDEO DOWNLOADER SOFTWARE (FREE) & SEARCH "TALKING BIRDS" & "BIRD TRAINING" VIDS ON YOUTUBE, DOWNLOAD ABOUT 10 OF THEM (make sure you screen them 1st to know that there is nothing on there you don't want your birds learning), SAVE THEM ON YOUR COMPUTER, OPEN WINDOWS MOVIE MAKER, UPLOAD EACH VID INTO A PROJECT FILE, SAVE PROJECT FILE, BURD DVD OF PROJECT & LET THE REAL FUN BEGIN. PLAY IT ONCE OR TWICE A DAY, MAKE IT AS LONG AS YOU WANT & TOTALLY CUSTOMIZED TO YOUR BIRD'S NEEDS, & BE AMAZED IN A FEW DAYS WHEN THEY ARE LETTING YOU HANDLE THEM, PET THEM, & THEY ARE BEGINNING TO SAY ALL SORTS OF FUNNY THINGS!... REMAKE NEW YOUTUBE MOVIE PROJECTS FOR YOUR BIRDS AS NEEDED. WE HAVE LIKE 10 OF THEM & THEY HAVE HELPED A LOT MORE THAN THIS "UNVENTURE PARROT" DVD!!!! (*Then thank me later for this money saving tip that actually works... oh & here's another tip, find GoodBird Inc.'s channel cuz they have tons of funny & educational vids... done by a REAL bird behavior expert named Barbara whom I was referred to! And she's super nice & will return your email questions & actually puts out GOOD & worthwhile bird training vids w/real tips on everthing from when to do training sessions, to finding a good diet, troubleshooting behavior problems, & teaching them to talk! *And NO I don't even know anyone at GoodBird Inc personally & was not put up to endorsing them at all! I just believe in being HONEST & when a DVD is this bad & I feel like I was ripped off, then I thinks its my responsibility as a good honest person to let people know not to make the same mistake & what else is better out there! I've ordered GoodBird Inc's stuff too & just began corresponding w/Barbara, & was really impressed by her wisdom, insight, tips, & all the free stuff she puts out on youtube... this Adventure Parrot DVD isn't even worthy of a free app in iTunes!!!! Whoever made this needs to either get some more education on bird training & behavior, OR just get real honest w/themselves about what kind of "services/products" they are selling & whether or not its more about them making as much money as possible for as little time & effort as possible, vs making a quality product. I'm just livid I spent money on this junk & a friggin Petco employee could have taught me more than this... a friggin illegal bird smuggler could have had better insight into taming birds! Geesh (& yes that was a joke... I do NOT endorse illegal activity or the illegal bird trade. Either buy a handfed well reared baby, OR consider adopting a 2nd chance bird from a shelter like we did! And save money by making your own recordings for your birds from youtube vids (which my birds LOVE, like the famous African Grey Einstein who doesn't swear & knows tons of phrases) & also you can make your own audio recording of your voice repeating the words & phrases you want your birds to learn too, either on your smartphone's voice recorder feature, or even on your computer, or a blank tape & can make it as long as you want for them to have something REALLY educational to learn while you're at work!!! Unless your bird knows how to use a remote, the most they'll see is one 5 min boring section of this DVD then have nuthing else to do for the rest of the day!

My birds usually LOVE watching tv, especially of other birds doing & saying funny things (even the nature scenes looked like they were done on an old outdated camera phone) & they both thought this was awful... as evident by both of them literally getting up & walking away from the tv to go back & play on their parrot park, after the 1st 30 seconds! Then when I picked the "Perch with a View" section they ran & hid!!! (now I worry that they'll be traumatized any time I try to get them to watch anything REALLY genuinely educational again, for fear of giant raccoons attacking them, which also did NOT help when it came to the "taming your parrot" sections cuz they already don't like to be held, let alone after fearing a wild raccoon is gonna come get them if they come out of hiding! Way to make a hit guys!!! I'll send you the bill from the bird behaviorist I'll no doubt be hiring to help my poor babies now! Birds are smart & everything to them is interactive... including tv, which means anyone who owns birds knows to make sure its something that will help foster trust & learning in positive ways, carefully thinking through each scene & going thru all footage to ask "is this something a bird would enjoy? Would it scare them? Will it help them to really honestly learn? Is it role-modeling positive behavior? Is it teaching good things? Is it helpful for the owners? Does it genuinely make a bird's dull day alone a bit brighter? Is it WORTH THE MONEY WE WILL BE CHARGING? Is the footage good quality using good equipment that looks professional or does it look like something the lady writing this review could shoot in her backyard with her 4 plants on her porch using her camera phone?" Those are the hard questions a good video maker asks themselves when entering a business venture like this. So don't lie to yourselves (& your paying viewers) by marketing this as something its NOT, then wondering why you get bad reviews. My guess is the makers just don't really care about the reviews or feedback, but only care about the bit of money they think they can make before word gets around cyberspace that their material sucks! (Which I cannot condone & think is shady as hell & thus why I'm trying to drive this point home to the makers in this lengthy review! Cuz moral ambiguity may be for Donald Trump, but most would agree that it doesn't fly w/pet owners, OR more specifically bird owners, who know how smart their birds are & what they want their birds to know. We already spent enough money on our birds, the supplies & care, the vet, etc... birds are one of the most expensive pets one can own, so after sinking all that money into our beloved feathered friends, we want to give them the quality live & learning that will ensure a long lifetime as a member of the family! I used to think that bird people are just nuts! I didn't understand how a bird owner feels about their "babies." But now I get it, & I'm proud to be one of those crazy bird people who thinks my bird are the bests friend one could ever have! So I hope more bird owners stand up when they see this kind of junk & corrupt mindless idiots trying to make a buck off our love for our pets! When a bird owner (whose already sank a ton into their bird) spends even more money on buying them an educational DVD, that means they truly care about their bird at any cost, & should NOT be taken advantage of! We buy this stuff for a reason & this reason which most of us have, is clearly NOT MET by this DVD at all (as evident by the other negative reviews). Don't take advantage of a bird owner's desire to help foster learning in their beloved friend, cuz that's just cruel... can you say CLASS ACTION?!!! Thats what happens to business owners who take advantage of paying customers & dupe them out of money, & this DVD was NOT made with ANY education whatsoever in mind for birds... its clearly just an attempt to make a quick buck at our expense & that to me is the worst of all evils!!!! The makers of this crap should be ashamed of themselves for thinking this low quality footage could pass as an educational video or anything else they tried to sell it as! When you sell a product under false guises then you have only manipulated your buyers & that is immoral & I hope God punishes you for trying to pass this junk off as anything you could even sell! If I coulda rated this Negative 100 stars I would have!

I cannot state enough was a VERY foolish & stupid move to put a raccoon or any other predatory animal on a video birds would be watching & trying to learn from in a safe environment, & is something only a truly naive bird owners would make, not thinking something like that through, nor even trying to come up with a few more interesting scenes like a flock of wild birds playing in a tree (which is what I'd hoped that section would have to mimicked watching out the window, which proved to be more interesting & fun for them). So its officially been Bird Test & Birdie Rated at "0 stars!" (so if my own birds both hated it, you KNOW its a waste of money... they've learned more from that car insurance commercial of the dude who bought the falcon!... at least they learned to make falcon calls from that & watched the whole thing! Lol. So Sorry Pet Media, but this is a serious flop of epic proportions! Better luck next time... or maybe you should just consider getting out of the video making biz all together (and don't even think about doing a book or any other source of media! Go back to college or go work at a zoo!) Oh & my birds both say "Stupid Looser" (which they don't say unless they are either really upset or disappointed, or really just didn't like something! Oh & they learned this long before I got them, so I did not teach them this... but at least they reserve their potty-mouths for actual real dismal situations!) I would apologize for my harsh words, but words cannot even begin to describe how horrible this DVD is!!!!!!! ...literally! It seems even the makers of the vid were "at a loss for words!" Hahahahaha, I made a funny =)

Anyhow I hope this helps future shoppers to avoid purchasing this DVD, cuz if there was even ONE good thing about it, I'd have said it, cuz I try to see the good in everything... but this was 8 short sections of nothingness & pure torture for both me & my birds! Unless your birds need to learn how to preen themselves or each other or have a deep desire to see babies (and not even cute hatchling babies) get syringe-fed & vomit, there is no reason whatsoever to get this piece of crap!!!!! Or you'll be the next to leave a bad review!

top_profane.text:
Would recommend, have bought several times, and will definitely buy again!

We have two dogs, and an indoor-only cat. I scoop up a lot of poop around here, and go through quite a few plastic grocery bags.

After seeing a short video on a social media site about the plastic trash “islands” out in the oceans, and how plastic grocery bags do such damage to marine life, I sat and thought about how many we actually use and toss away. I’d not given it a lot of thought before because, at least, I was using each grocery bag more than once. Once to bring the groceries home and then again as trash bags for small cans, to pick up dog poop in the yard, and to dump the used cat litter after daily scooping.

But they’re still plastic bags that, hopefully, end up in the land fill. They still could end up in the local bodies of water, rivers, and streams.

How could I use less?

I went in search of. Considering those grocery bags are “free,” I wanted something that was economical (we’re not made of money), as well as better for the environment. Too many of the pet waste bags are still made of some type of plastic even though they say they’re environmentally or earth friendly. Plastic is not.

Then I spotted Doggy Do Good. I read their descriptions and went to their site to read more about them. They make vegetable-based bags that biodegrade, not just break down into smaller particles of plastic, and they’re compostable (industrial compost, not home compost). 10% of their profits go to help dogs in no-kill shelters. Made in America! Even though they’re more expensive per bag than others, I bought a box of the “XL Cat Litter/X-Large Dog Waste Bags” to give them a try.

First off, they do not have a chemical odor. They have a silky feel to me, which is not unpleasant at all. It is not difficult to remove them from the roll, though I do take some care when removing the next one so as not to take a chance on tearing them. I decided to use them in my master bath trash can, which is a 2-gallon (8-quart) galvanized bucket: top diameter 9 ¾ inches by 8 3/8 inches tall. It is emptied about every week to week-and-a-half. The Doggy Do Good bags fit in it very well.

I also use them in the trash can I use when scooping out the cat’s litter box. My only problem with the bags is they don’t quite fit so well in a regular, 4-gallon trash can. The blue oval trash can (pictured) is 12 ¼ inches wide by 6 ½ inches, and it is 10 ½ inches tall. The bags are a bit short. To combat that, I tug up the one side of the bag and pull it over the lip of the trash can. I prop that side against the side edge of the litter box to hold it in place as I am scooping. Which fascinates the cat, by the way. To use less bags, I’ll leave that bag in the trash can for a few days or so of poop-scooping before I dispose of the whole thing. I use clumping clay cat litter, and even after up to five days of poop and pee litter disposal, the Doggy Do Good bags have held the weight, and not torn or ripped out, when I tie it off and carry it out to the big 30-gallon trash can in the garage.

Because of their more than decent performance, I bought more of the XL bags. I keep a box in my bathroom and in the “cat room.”

We recently decided we should go camping and bought a motor home (Class A) RV. Nothin’ quite like *camping* with your own bed, air conditioning, and potty!

We’ve been to two pet-friendly state parks within our home state so far. They, very thoughtfully and conveniently, have pet poop bag stations scattered about, but those bags are plastic! I bought another box of the XL Doggy Do Good bags to keep in the RV to use in the little trash can we have in our RV bathroom, and for poop picking up. Unfortunately, they just aren’t big enough to fit properly in the trash can we have under the kitchen sink.

Our daughter, who lives with us, brought along her little poop bag dispenser for when we walked the dogs (multiple times per day), but at our second camping expedition, we ran out of those little bags! Luckily, I had the larger Doggy Do Good bags, and there were the park-offered bags here and there.

Because I am making a conscious effort to use less plastic, and I like the bags, I bought the little Doggy Do Good poop bags and a dispenser to clip on my own dog’s harness or leash!

So, yes. They are more expensive than other poop bags, or small trash bags, they don’t quite fit in a 4-gallon small trash can, but if you exert a bit of effort, that can be worked around. They are convenient as heck; can easily be carried with you when walking your dog; hold the weight of clay cat litter scoopages of several days; contain the odor; they’re not plastic and truly are biodegradable, and the company donates to shelters! Sounds like a win/win.

However, I will say I have not done any sort of test to see how long they will hold a liquid.

Pros: listed above

Cons: The expense, and the XL bags could be a bit larger to fit better in small, vanity or bathroom type trash cans.
top_profane.title: It’s so difficult to use less plastic. Doggy Do Good really helps! Made in the USA

top_profane.text:
This is overpriced, tiny, and messy.

Let's start with the prices. They give away the pail for next to nothing. But you'd have to pay exorbitant amounts for the bags. At $6 per bag, and using the manufacturer's numbers for capacity, you'd pay $144 per year for bags, for one average size cat. You'd empty it once every two weeks. If you used regular trash bags (Glad, Hefty), you'd spend $4 per year. That's $140 you're throwing away. And no, Litter Genie bags don't provide better tear protection. The maybe 10 lb of litter you'd get per 2-week period won't strain an ordinary trash bag.

Litter Genie pails are tiny. The opening's barely as large as a litter scoop. It's impossible to avoid spilling litter outside the pail.

In addition, you're supposed to pull a handle every time you use it. Disgusting. I definitely want to avoid touching trash cans as much as possible.

Odors aren't a real issue with regular lidded trash cans anyway. As long as you get a reasonable cat litter (just not the cheapest kind), you won't have an issue with odor. The Litter Genie might be better at locking away odors, but it's solving an issue that doesn't really exist.

I use Simplehuman 8 gallon trash cans with Simplehuman Code J bags. The lids are operated by foot pedals, so I don't need to touch the trash can every time I use it. The trash cans come with removable inner trays, which I can use to carry the bags out, so there's no risk of the bag tearing. Their trash bags can hold 60 lb of litter easily without tearing or stretching, in case I do want to take the bags out of the tray. In fact, I've never seen a Simplehuman trash bag tear in the real world. I've only gotten them to tear when I placed several steel weight lifting plates in them, but those are several times denser than cat litter. The opening is way larger than a Litter Genie's--there's basically no chance of spilling any litter. In case you can lift more than 10 lb at a time, you won't have to worry about emptying the trash can for months at a time. They're regular trash cans too, so you can throw your regular trash in them if you wanted to. And the cost? $100 for the trash can, but just $1 per year for bags, in the same scenario as above. You'd break even in about 8 months.
top_profane.title: Overpriced and tiny

I hardly ever give only one star for the same reason 5 stars at precious. Nothing is that bad or that good. In this case it really is that bad. The materials and quality of assembly seem to be okay, but the design

I'm admittedly cranky right now, having spent half a day and way more than spare change to accomplish an installation that is riddled with obstacles.
Enough of the niceities. I'm pissed and not at myself. Petsafe markets this as being universal but if you intend to do a wall installation you better have a ton of tools and a bunch of common sense. If you are short on either don't even attempt it. I have both and I'm still frustated. My home is 13 years old and is of frame stucco construction. Here are the layers, outside in. 3/4 in stucco, which is a cement compound and requires masonary tools, 3/4 to1 inch styrofoam which requires a vacuum to contain the mess, chicken wire, while great for holding stucco to styrofoam is a bitch to saw, plywood or particle board, both wood products and fairly easy to work with, insulation, and finally, drywall. If you do the math, the composit can vary more than a little. For some very sensible reasons I chose to do the wall installation, and I found out that it really isn't made to be a wall mount. They threw a kit together, charged a hundred bucks for it, and it may or may not work for you. It didn't work for me. My very typical stucco construction, at least in the west, apparently not considered, was too much for the wall mount kit. The horribly overpriced kit's least expensive item was a piece of plastic that was intended to fill the gap between the main indoor unit and the out door trim. In my case it was two inches too narrow. A cheap piece of crap plastic and they were stingy with it. It can be trimmed with scissors, give us too much. With the large door and the additional wall kit I spent over $200 at Amazon and still made two trips to my local Ace hardware so Im reasonably miffed that Pet
safe shorted me .75 cnts worth of Chinese plastic. So now I'm in for $250 and I havene to invent my own filler. Is there anybody at Petsafe that doesn't think this is okay? Lastly, though my install is only 3/4 done, I have one more gripe. I only have one stud that I can use so I put the indoor unit in the hole and shot a screw on the side of the stud and hit nothing. I'm too pissed and too tired to find out why but common sense says the mounting holes are too close to the edge. A 2x4 is at least 1 3/4 inches so how hard is it to hit that target. I shaved the opening twice to get it big enough to fit the door in with some persuasion so how could I not have a screw find wood?
The people that marketed this item didn't field test it with real people and because of that, they haven't found all the faults or made any attempts to correct the ones pointed out by their customers. I made one support call prior to the installation to check about improvements that were needed according to the Amazon customers and the person I talked to said no improvements have been made or planned but she was sure to point out the warranty.They have design flaws that the most generous warranty won't correct. It's a piece of crap that won't work ever but they'll gladly send you another one, for a year. After that you're on your own. Totally disgusted with companies that won't improve their product, especially one like this that so desperately needs it.

top_rant.text:
Ok, so I bought one of these fancy new 306T heat-temperature controller things. Now most folks that know me well would say somethin' like "Now what the dickens is that boy gonna do with that!?". Well, down here in the south it gets hot, real hot. Most of our energy-conservation is aimed at keeping our pretty little heads (and other parts) COOL. Yeah, the heat and humidity down here is rather tough. We claim we can cut a chunk off with a knife and send it to Alaska and it'll still be solid when it gets there. Yeah, hot and humid enough to make the big pole at the post office wilt.

So, what's a guy from the enchanted land of humidity supposed to do with something that controls a heating device? Can't plug it into our nearest star to turn it's temp down when it gets too hot. Nope, that won't work, besides...I don't have an extension cord that long (got a bunch of'em, though!). I don't hatch chickens and I don't own an iguana, though I have an old highschool buddy that resembles one strongly (that's another story). So....

Down here in the deep south hardly anybody knows what a “frost line” is. What, bury a water line three feet deep? Shoot put it down six inches so you won't run over it with the lawn mower and forget it!!!! Bare pipes running everywhere, exposed to the heat...and the occasional hard freeze we get some winters. Man, when that little weatherman guy comes on and starts talking about sub-freezing temperatures you can meet the whole town down at Joe Maynard's hardware store buying up pipe insulation, heat lamps, propane torches (to thaw them frozen pipes out later, of course!...and later we hear about so-n-so (can't say his name here) burning his garage down where he had been making his whiskey while he was thawing pipes, but again, that's another story).

Yeah, people start scampering around like my 3-year old granddaughter running back from the kitchen with a crazed look in her eyes and a Popsicle she just snuck from the freezer hanging out her mouth! Suddenly everybody is panicking trying to protect those delicate pipes that they swore last year they were going to “take care of”. I'm usually right there with'em. Sometimes it gets right dangerous with so many desperate people vying for that last heat tape. Watch out for them little old ladies with the blue hair...they can get mighty mean when the pipe insulation starts running low. Humanity can be so cold at times...

So here's what I've done. I'm just as guilty as the next fella about procrastinating about getting my pipes permanently insulated. I usually run around with the herd of folks looking for those foam tubes for insulation and fiberglass wrapping stuff for the crooked places. You ever seen two old pharts playing tug-o-war with a piece of foam pipe insulation??? Oh, and my favorite....Infra Red heat lamps....they do work a treat in keeping those pipes from freezing. I've got one really bad area that I do my best to protect...it's the outside washer and hot water heater!!! Open ended garage...exposed pipes. Yeah, it's kind of open to the elements. Why, I do believe that was where my wife come face to face (the first time) with a fat ol' possum....them things are ugly and even uglier when they “hiss” at you...being from a large city she really didn't know how to take that possum but I think she took the cow drinking out of the bird bath a bit better, but I digress. Man, I *hate* plumbing work (but ain't got nothin' aginst possums) so I really couldn't make it without those heat lamps!!

But, there's a problem with those heat lamps...they don't have a brain to turn on and off when they need to. And I'm at work a lot of times when they need to turn on...or off. Don't need'em running needlessly just to send my do-reh-me to the greedy power-company. And I don't need my pipes ah freezing 'cause I wasn't there to turn the lamps on. I just need those heat lamps a burning when it's freezing, that's all. But I can't be in two places at once and there's a not a friendly troll that comes along and unplugs them for me....

DRUM ROLL PLEASE!!!!! Enter in the 306T controller!!! You ever see them old science fiction movies where the aliens all looked like they had one of those beehive hairdos but instead it was their brain? These were mostly black and white movies...real grainy and we had to twist the outdoor antenna to get'em to come in good. Well anyhow, the 306T would look like that if it was an alien 'cause it'd have a really big brain!!! Really smart contraption. What can I say, eh?

Ok, so here's what I did. I took the 306T and hooked those 250W heat lamps up to it. Set a differential of a couple of degrees off from 32F and let her rip. Works like charm!!! No more spinning that meter like it's a Chuck Berry 45rpm record that the electric-company's doing “The Twist” to!!! Stops them blood-suckers from tippy-toeing through my wallet!!!! Yessire!!!! When it's not cold enough to freeze the pipes the 306T turns off the heat lamps, when it gets close to freezing the 306T turns them back on. No frozen pipes!!!! No trips to the hardware store trying to match up pipes and fittin's and swearing that I'll get everything honky-dory by next winter...no WWE fights with little ol' blue-haired ladies.

Nope, the 306T doesn't hardly lift it's pinky-finger and turns those heat lamps on-and-off, on-and-off, on-and-off...and me, well I'm sittin' inside drinking a warm cup of something that warms me up or a cold cup of something that warms me up. I can set my thoughts on more important things...like finding that alien movie with the big-brained space aliens in it. Now that's somethng positive to be doing rather than pluggin' in and unpluggin' heat lamps...let the brain do it for ya!!!! Now, what'd I do with the tv remote control....maybe the 306T could find it for me....it's the real brain around here!!!!!!!!! Dang fine piece of equipment, too!!! NASA could take some tips from this Inkbird company. Must be a bunch of them big-brained Aliens running it cause this this controller is smarter'n Doctor Spock was and he was always amazing Scotty and Scotty was pretty smart himself!!! Fine company of aliens, I guess. At least they're friendly and won't eat'ya.

So, if your hatching ostriches or maybe raising salwater crocs to hand out to the kids at Halloween and you're having trouble keeping the inkubators at the right temperature then fret no more!!!! You can get that temperature honed in to a “T” with a nice and shiny Inkbird 306T!!! And then when you get them ostriches and crocodiles all hatched out and happy and winter starts to move in you can keep your pipes from freezing with it, too!!!!! Think of all your money that you can keep out of the electric-company's hands!!! Shazamm!!!! It brangs tears to my eyes I tell ya!!!! It's an amazing piece of technology!!!!...something that keeps me from having to do plumbing work! The president oughta give ol Inkbird a medal!!!!!!!!!!!!!

Customer service is great!!! It's the answer to all your problems. You can contact them if you do have a problem. I was having trouble baking pound cakes, they was just coming out all wrong and not airy like Miss Linda's cakes come out. I'd sifted the flour just right. I didn't stomp around in the kitchen with my work boots on. And still it'd come out a big flop. I contacted customer service and and this nice person went through the steps and things with me. Ended up I wasn't lettin' the eggs and butter sit out over night and come to room temperature like I was supposed to be doing. Amazing customer service!!!!!! Inkbird is a class act, I tell ya!!!

Now, you know you want an Inkbird 306T, you really want one bad! Jerry Pickler down the road from you, he lives in the green house with all the funny-looking antennas on it...over behind where the feedstore used to be 'fore it burned down back in '73...hmmm, might've been '74. I think they was using a propane torch to thaw some frozen pipes out...shame Inkbird wasn't around back then. Anyhow, I'll bet Jerry's got one of the 306T controllers for inkubating his emus!!! (Jerry has some fine emus, too!) Don't let Jerry outsmart you even if he has twelve toes and can climb a tree better'n anybody in the county (has held the county tree climbing championship for eleven years runnin'!!!). And last thing, I really haven't seen him at the hardware running around like a maniac the last few freezes that we've had. Jerry Pickler ain't smarter'n you are...is he? Nuff said.

Ya know, I got to wondering just now. My feet tend to get cold in the winter when I'm sleepin'....yeah, you know what I'm thinking...see, you're getting the “Inkbird Insight”!!! Yeah, a little duck tape and tape that probe to my a big toe and plug my 'lectric heater up. YEAH BOY!!! TROPICAL PARADISE!!!! This 306T is an amazing contraption, like an electronic Swiss army knife!!!!...musta been one of them fizzicist scientist (or a really smart big-brained alien) that figured this thing out!!!

Oh well, I gotta go. Got me something cool to drink and I think “Lost in Space” is coming on the television (I think one of them big-brained aliens is supposed to be in this epysode)...gotta adjust the antenna just a tad, too. It's amazing, I thought the show had ended years ago but it's still coming on...and Will Robinson hasn't aged a bit!!!! Weird!!!! Oh.....now where'd I put my meds?......they're here somewhere....oh, here they are....hmm, I don't remember that one, oh well... All set to relax since I ain't wrappin' pipes for the winter and I know this purdy Inkbird 306T is gonna take care of things!!!!!!! YEEEHAWWWW!!!
