top_rant.text:
Now obviously this is quite a long review, but I try to address most of the questions that are being asked here in the main description and want you to make an informed decision of what you are getting, especially since you will be most likely taking care of a live pet in this and want something both attractive and a healthy place for your pet in the long term. So here goes:

I switched my Betta, Sushi, from a tetra 1.5gallon tank [[ASIN:B0018CFMYS Tetra 29137 WaterWonders Aquarium Cube, 1.5 Gallons, Black]] because with the added heater in that small volume of water, it was raising temps too high. You can click on my name to see review of that unit and see pictures of how I generally setup this 3 gallon unit.

For this 3 gallon unit as is, out of the box, I cannot recommend this tank (see CONS) >>BUT<< if you are willing to spend some more on upgrading what comes with this unit, you can have quite a good and attractive tank using this tank as a starting point. The PROS and CONS I list here (as well as the 2 out of 5 stars rating I gave it) will deal with just the tank as supplied right out of the box. Let's start with the good stuff.

PROS:
1) Elegant design: I know this is subjective, but having no dark edge bezels is nicer in my opinion. The raised base is solid when water and gravel are added. It's nice to be able to see "all" of the tank with minimal breakup of the entire look from most angles.

2) Supplied clip on LED: Mine has 9 individual LEDs that all came working (no dead LEDs) and nicely and evenly disperses light at an apparent comfortable level that does not bother my Betta. Unlike the 1.5gallon version, this LED unit is completely separate with its own power cord and not fused to the filter. NOTE: I do not believe these LEDS are suitable for most live plants (no way to verify as I could not find any documentation in box/online at what Kelvin these LEDs are at). But based on price of other trusted aquarium LED plant lights, I doubt you are getting plant/photosynthetic capable LEDs with this unit.

3) CUTOUTS: Has in the back right (if tank is facing you)approximately 3inch wide x 2inch deep cutout that will fit an after market filter comfortably (which this tank is going to require as the supplied filter is not recommended, see CONS). In front, similar to the 1.5gallon version, has a square cutout making it extremely easy to feed fish. More importantly, all the cutouts are rounded which is a major plus as I've seen other acrylics at this price point come with roughly cut edges that can nick you.

4) SMALL FOOTPRINT: just a tad larger foot print than it's 1.5 gallon counterpart and one of the reasons I bought this particular unit. If you are looking for a nice but small accent aquarium that can be put on almost any stable surface that can handle at least 30lbs (water is about 8lb/gallon x3 + rocks + plants, etc), then this might be the aquarium for you. There are other similar acrylic aquariums sold here on Amazon, but most are not square / are rectangular / looks like they could take up more space. This is a nice compact design.

CONS:
1) FIT and FINISH: Not nearly as good as the 1.5gallon, which surprised me. The 1.5gallon version has a slightly curved top that fit flush with the edges. This one comes with a "flat" top. I put that in quotes because BOTH 3 gallon tanks I received actually had bowed /curved edges in the front that up close is noticeable and detracts from the neat elegant lines this tank is supposed to give off. So at eye level, you can see it plain as day open gaps in the front edges. (If I have time, I will upload pics of the bowed edges) Looking online, all pictures were flat and flush so I think this is a manufacturing defect and I ask anyone else who has this tank to chime in and let me know if they also received tanks with bent tops. Even after months, these edges remain up, they didn't settle down. As the material is acrylic I didn't want to chance putting on a heavy object to "flatten" the tops out. I cringe every time I clean the tank and see these edges. Very annoying.

2) INCLUDED FILTER and PUMP IS JUNK: many people will see free filter and included pump and think that's great. Well on BOTH the units I received, the pumps were OBNOXIOUSLY LOUD and one of them vibrated so badly near the tank it was visibly distressing my Betta. One pump was actually louder than my refrigerator when the the icemaker motor was making ice. That's just not acceptable. Do yourself a favor and once you open the box, just throw out that filter into the garbage. I'm dead serious. Also, since the pump is separate from the filter and connected via tubing, if the power goes out, you risk having water drain out of the tank onto the floor so you would be wise to buy a check valve if you insist on keeping the supplied crappy filter/pump. I suspect the only reason this cheap filter was thrown in was so that you would be locked into buying their branded replacement filters. Kind of like when razor blade company gives you a free razor so you you are locked into buying their blades. You would do much better for your fish and your sanity to buy an after market filter, I suggest and reviewed the Azoo Mignon 60 filter here: [[ASIN:B005VEWCMO AZOO Mignon Filter 60]] Also, the Azoo filter fits perfectly at the back cutout and even with the extension, will easily clear the ground floor on this 3 gallon tank.

3) Acrylic material: This is both a pro and a con. A pro because acrylic looks nice. A con in the long term because it scratches quite easily so you have to be really, really careful while cleaning this tank, even if you are using an acrylic approved sponge.

--------------------
OPTIONAL RECOMMENDED UPGRADES:
Well, if you've made it this far you might still be interested in buying this tank. Good. The following are suggestions on how you can pimp out this tank and make it really stand out and shine and make your fish more comfortable and remain healthy.

1) LIVE PLANTS: Now if you are going to just stick with the supplied LED light fixture then I can only recommend "set it and forget" plants that don't require (but it helps if you do) plant food and proper Kelvin rated light fixture. The following suggested plants should do well in low to medium light and this particular tank (they are thriving in mine with just the included LED lamp).

A) marimo moss ball
B) java MOSS, see me review here: [[ASIN:B00COXK74U Java Moss - Vesicularia Dubyana - Live Aquarium Plant (5x5sm)]]
C) java FERN (Microsorum Pteropus): there is a guy who sells it here on Amazon, but the price seems a bit high for what you are getting, so I suggest first checking out your local fish store and compare prices. I bought mine at local store.

NOTE: if you insist on using artificial plants then I suggest using silk plants (with no metal stems) or if you are at the pet store, make sure they pass the "panty hose test". As mentioned in my 1.5gallon review: Most people will be tempted to put in decorations, plants, etc into this tank and specific to Bettas, almost all of them will tear Betta fins or worse. Bring some panty hose with you to the pet store where you are thinking of buying your fixtures and if it easily catches and/or shreds the hose, it's probably not for this tank + Betta.

2) HEATER: If you are intending this tank for a Betta, then a heater is a must have. This is this one I am using in this tank: "Hydor 7.5w Slim Heater for Bettas" and reviewed here: [[ASIN:B006JVQ67K Hydor 7.5w Slim Heater for Bettas and Bowls up to 5 gal]]

3) PROPER FILTER with proper inline filter products:
As mentioned before, the Azoo Mignon 60 filter but that too needs a little work.
I added the following products:
A) Aqua Clear Biomax 20: [[ASIN:B0006JLO5K Aquaclear 20-Gallon Biomax]] which provides a massive surface area for "good" beneficial bacteria to culture and grow on. If you get the suggested filter, stick with Biomax 20 because I suspect anything larger won't fit in the filter chamber.
B) Aqua clear sponge insert: [[ASIN:B0002AQK48 Aquaclear 20-Gallon Foam Inserts, 3-Pack]]This higher quality sponge provides decent mechanical filtration i.e. traps all the big crap and junk from returning back into the tank and will provide some substrate for beneficial bacteria (not nearly as good as the Biomax though)

If I have time, I will upload more recent photos of the 3 gallon setup with a mix of live and silk plants. The tank looks quite good once "pimped" out and the water chemistry with usual water changes has been rock solid stable. Sushi is apparently very happy in the larger volume of water and is extremely bright and fins just look outstanding. I've received many compliments on this setup from family and friends who have come over. This is small enough to make a very attractive accent piece almost anywhere (don't recommend in direct sunlit areas).

I still have the 1.5 gallon tank setup with no fish in it. I use it as an emergency hospital tank (hope I never need it for that purpose) but mostly just a place where Sushi is put when I'm doing the monthly thorough tear down clean of his main 3 gallon tank (remember this tank even with filter will require weekly water changes).

BOTTOM LINE: If you intend just to buy this as is and not put in the upgrades, I can't recommend this tank at all. However, if you are willing to upgrade and you are looking for a small footprint aquarium and can live with the (likely) bowed top you are going to get, then this becomes a STRONG BUY recommendation.

I know this was very long, but I hope it helps you. If you are willing to put in some extra money, I think most will be quite happy with this tank.

If anyone has any questions, feel free to shoot them at me, I will try to get back to you if I notice them.
top_rant.title: Ugly duckling out of box but IF you are willing to spend more on upgrades, you'll have a swan

top_rant.text:
This is a really great cat tree! I'm very impressed with the quality and design. Yes, you have to assemble it yourself and the instructions leave a little to be desired -- there's 18 individual pieces plus all the hardware (screws, brackets, etc.) for putting those 18 pieces together, yet there's only 5 little images of how all those pieces and parts and hardware go together! I definitely had some moments of confusion in the assembly process, but I got there eventually. (And I put together lots of things, so it's not like I'm not used to these sorts of things. I can imagine it being a challenge for less experienced self-assemblers!) But the product itself is great! The carpet is SO VERY soft! I searched for months, hunting the right cat tree for my "outdoor-turned-indoor" (moving from rural to urban environment) cat. I saw so many cat trees that had what I call "sheepskin fur" covering. And what I always find with that material (sheep fur) is that it's nearly bald in between the "poofy" areas and wears out or tears very quickly, so I wanted to avoid anything with that type of outer layer. Moving on to "carpet covered" trees...I found a lot of them seemed to be very ill-fitting, with lumps & bumps on areas that should be perfectly smooth. So what is left? This stuff!!!! This covering is not carpet or sheepskin, but like a blanket. It's not that thick (one small negative) and I have no idea how it will hold up for the long-haul, but it's so darn soft that I don't care at this point!!!! There are several attribute of this particular tree & manufacturer that I want to point out {I'll try to go from bottom of [assembled] cat tree to top}:
1. The base -- It's heavier and thicker than the upper levels, an important factor for sturdiness, I would think. So that's a good thing.
2. The "self-brushing" kitty arch on the base -- I really wanted a tree with this feature. Even before I put it together, my cat and I were testing out this piece, with me pulling the arched bristles over kitty's back. She loved it. I suspect she'll love it even more once she realizes it's there!!!
3. The RAMP -- This was another feature that was incredibly important to me. Yes, I'm aware that cats can & do climb ladders. Perhaps some even love doing it. But I have never ever seen MY cat use a ladder. A lot of the kitty trees on the market have "ladder-style" devices to get from level to level. I didn't want that for my cat. Not only am I unsure of how she'd react to using a ladder, but I also thought of her later in life. What if she's 18 years old, a little feeble, less self-assured? Would walking up "fall through" rungs be a good idea? I think not. No, give me and my cat a nice solid ramp to use. This was a serious consideration for me and it was hard to find a tree with this feature, plus all the others I wanted.
4. "Kitty condo" on the 2nd tier -- Again, a lot of other kitty trees had really shoddy looking condos, especially those sheepskin ones. They appeared to be near-transparent on the walls and flimsy all around. This condo comes with two interior supporting rods that you put together. It's not the largest condo in the world and might not fit larger cats, but my cat is in the 8lb. range, and this condo will work for her, IF she ever decides to use it. (She's never been a "hide inside of anything" cat -- no boxes, bags, blankets...or condos, apparently!)
5. Rope toy -- my cat has never had a device like this in her life, and we're still in the "what is it and do I trust it" phase, but knowing her the way I do, I'm sure in about 2 weeks, this cat tree will be "all the rage". Having the hanging rope toy on the 2nd level is something I think she will come to enjoy, once she makes her way up there!!!
6. Kitty "nest" -- I know this is a feature some cats love and others simply ignore. Mine falls into the IGNORE category. But, maybe as she ages (she's still quite young, around 4 years old), perhaps her tastes will change. Whatever the case, it's there if my cat, or yours, wants to use it. And if you're, for some reason, totally against giving them the "nest" or, as I call it, "hammock", option, you could opt to not install it at all. And I like that. I may end up taking it off our tree, if I find it's more of a nuisance than it's worth and it becomes clear that my cat will never step paw into it. And, it also swivels, so you can turn it out of the way of "foot traffic" walking past the cat tree, if necessary.
7. A water/food/treat bowl!?!?!?!!?!? WHAT??? Who is the genius that thought of this! I want to shake their hand! This was a huge "oh, I'm definitely buying this one" factor for me. I just loved the fact that someone thought of this. Again, whether MY cat ever takes advantage of it is still an unknown, but I have put a couple crunchy kitty treats in that bowl, just in case! I'll probably fill it with water in the summer (it's currently January in New England --- if I could turn it into a space heater, I probably would! LOL!). And my biggest surprise of all with this entire experience? The stainless steel bowl shown in the pictures? IT'S INCLUDED! I nearly fell over when I discovered that!!! They really thought of everything!!!!
8. And my favorite feature (or, at least, my equally favorite with the bowl) --- The top perch. This thing amazed me from the moment I saw it. "Is that thing shaped like a kitty ears?", I asked. Yes. Yes it is. I know everyone is going to ask, so I'll go ahead and mention it -- the round perch at the very top is 14.5 inches across the center of the circle (aka the Diameter). Anyway, it's very plush and those little "kitty ears"? I knew from the moment I saw them in the photos -- those are meant for cats to rub their cheeks on!!!! I've had over 50 cats of my own, and I've known more cats than I can count, and all of them did one thing in common: Rub their little faces on the corners of things, or against fingers, pens, phones, toes....WHATEVER! It's just one of those "cat things". Maybe I'm wrong, maybe the designers did not intend those kitty ears to be used for that, but I'm pretty sure that's what MY cat will use them for!!!! And that was it! I had to buy THIS cat tree!!!!
Of course, there's also the sisal scratching rope covering many of the poles.
Obviously, I hold this item in high regard. I hope this will help others, who are doing what I did and are searching high & low for that PURR-fect cat tree for the fur-babies. You can get all the other details, such as total height & width, from the description, but what you can't get is what the surface feels like (so soft that I want to sleep on it myself! Alas, I'm just too big!), or the little thoughts that were given to every detail (sure, anyone can cut a hole in a piece of wood and say it's for a water bowl...but how few manufacturers INCLUDE the bowl???), and how it feels like CATS DESIGNED IT. The price of this item is easily half that of many others I've researched, and those others don't even come close to being a fair comparison to this unit. This cat tree is one of the best purchases I think I've ever made.
Good luck to all the cat parents out there!!!!

top_rant.text:
***Update**** From 4 Stars to 1 ****
Tank will not lay directly on the bed. We have to use a twin blanket over it, and put his toys in it (probably don’t have to put his favorite chew time toys, but he loves toys in the bed).
Tank is a “blue nosed/blue dog, and he’s allergic to a good mumber of things. I get the feeling that whatever the cover is made out of makes him itchy/uncomfortable. I am sorry, but a dog Tanks size will take near anything to lie down on because he is not allowed on the furniture (I have enough kids already messing those up)
I’d also like to point out that the sides are not taller than the middle, but are the same height who’s is kinda a waste. Who wants a cuddle spot without sides to cuddle?
In the short of it - we’re back on the market for a better bed for our much Love Tankster.

We absolutely do NOT recommend this bed unless you change the cover.
&
We hope our review is helpful to you.

This will be a progressive review with appropriate updates;
Let me begin by saying Tank (the Momma’s dog) has been one of the absolutely best dogs I have ever had. At 120-130lbs Tank is a Razor’s Edge American Bully XL in the original style breeding. Which means he is a giant lovebug who absolutely adores his family! Tank is the kind of dog who not only does anything & everything we ask from him he also tries to please without being asked. Yeah - that kind of dog. Unshakable, and unfazed you can take him anywhere.
I have a condition that occasionally causes me to fall. Tank always seems to know when it’s going to happen, and will position himself under my arms, and will brace himself to help break my fall. He’s never been trained, or asked to do this. It’s just Tank taking care of his family. He never pulls the lead when I am unsteady, and yet will pull the husband, and our grown children in an effort to get them to go where he wants, but never hard enough to drag them - which he ever so easily could do! Hahaha! You (a stranger)could tell him all day long to “Sit”, and he’ll suddenly not understand English; However our 15 month old Grandbaby says something close to “ship!” while pointing at him, and he ever so happily sits, just waiting for what she wants next, and to let her know he understands! Hahaha! God Love this dog, Cause we absolutely do!!
So I think you get an idea how much our dog is loved. Needless to say we are not easily deterred by price tags when it comes to his health, happiness, & comfort. If that’s how much it costs that’s how much it costs.
We also have a large family, small hobby farm, and 4 other dogs.
**Everything we buy- that we would like to last so we’re not buying again -I take the time to learn about - such as what makes one more expensive than the other, which ones last longer, & attention to quality has been made.
**We had been buying all the dogs new beds every time they went on sale (every couple months), & have never really put any thought into it
** You know the kind I am sure - the big pillows in the big bin on the end of the isle at the feed store & pet stores.
**We have a totally a farm dog Jack Russell, a border collie x dalamation “boy’s dog” who loves the rough and tumble life that comes with following boy’s around, a now geriatric Old English Bulldogee who still reminds the other dogs who was here first (hehehe! She’s a good girl!), a Rhodesian ridgeback x bully mix who thinks all pillows need fluffing, and then of course Tank who needs extra because his weight is all in one area, & not spread out like say - a St. Bernard. The extra is needed just to keep his elbows and weight off the ground/ floor.
**SO those stupid pillows wear out within weeks - even when you buy everyone their own! :P
***During my shopping/comparing the very first thing I did was look at inner construction of the expensive “10 year guaranteed” dog beds.
**What I found was the actual memory foam is between 1& 1/2” - 3” thick. The rest is a hard foam they call “support foam” and similar names such as that - which is what they are actually guaranteeing will last for 10 years (**compare actual thickness of the support foam, and how much thickness they say their bed will retain for 10years) **There’s also a wide range of memory foams. Some will break down faster after time, some will last longer of course, **however even once they have lost their ability to keep expanding, and have broken down there will still be a half inch to full inch of material remaining to add to the thickness, and thereby you will be unable to warranty it out.
***+The big thing that stood out to me is how very ridged that base foam is! I imagine like sleeping on a rock after the memory foam topper breaks down.
****No thank you! I would rather buy another bed in a couple years which compared to most beds is an unheard of amount of time for big dogs! I do not wish to spend an extra hundred so Tank the Momma’s Dog can lay on a super hard bed in a couple years. :P

***Then it’s the bed covering materials- now that IS where I have to hand it to the $150-300.00 beds - those are going to last a good long time.
***The “ballistic” ones & “Bully” ones look like that may very well last their guaranteed 10yrs, and if not it’s not likely most people will retain their receipts long enough to actually be warrantied out.
I have to say I don’t actually mind paying $200 a dog bed, and a $1,000 for all 5 of our canine children (still cheaper than school clothes/books! Lol!) mostly because the dogs Never talk back ... well, Honey does, but she makes us laugh when she does!
**** BUT like I said I am not willing to pay premium prices when I can buy just as good for less. Matter of a fact I don’t know any parents with 4 or more kids who don’t do their “homework”. After a while it becomes a habit. ;)
Any who -
**** Whay I am seeing from this bed is DEFINITELY an inferior cover. Ugh! Tanks going to most likely thin it out, or rip it the first time he decides with his big paws (which means big claws) to “fluff” it.
***A problem I was aware of from other reviews before I purchased it, so-
***I bought him a “PAWZ Road” blanket to go with it (I know want one big enough for ME! This blanket is awesome!!).
** As long as he has a blanket to fluff on the cold nights (he’s a short hair - I love a COLD bedroom) I think this will do just fine.
***Most of the expensive bed have between 1 & 1/2” memory foam to 3” with a total foam height between 3”-7”.
**The only reason to buy a bed between 4” the 7” height is for big dogs to keep their joints, & bodies from sinking to the ground-
**memory foam always needs a firm base because it is VERY soft, and molds around the weight put on it.

***All that being said- this bed is in the medium range, and has the added height or the sides of the bed to cuddle against & generate a warm spot with their own body heat, or to rest their head on like a pillow (I have 3 pillow lovers, so it is important to us to have that feature. Plus the cold room factor).
***We do have experience with memory foam people beds, mattress toppers, and crib mattress toppers, but not 1 has taken this long to fill out once unpackaged.
**Just as an experiment (and because we have never had a mattress come already encased in a waterproof cover)
I went ahead and unzipped the waterproof cover on the cushion, and left the sides zipped up. The cushion is fluffing up on that side a lot faster than the “bolsters”/sides.
**However it is still taking for forever, so definitely not something you will be able to use right away.
*** If you are giving it as a gift I would suggest you open it a day, or 2 prior to the event, and then wrap it, or put a big bow & maybe a nice dog blanket on top of it - so the cover will last longer too!

***I am going to include some pictures at this time of what it looks like once it’s out of the box. Tank most likely will not have his new bed tonight, just his new blanket for now.
***Now that I have opened the watertight covering for the sides they are expanding pretty quick. The middle is still taking it’s sweet time an hour and a half later!!

***Our next update will be in a month. ***The following update will be done once the cushions either fail, or the cover does. My money is on the cover.

***We have also ordered 4 more beds in this, and 2 other brands, but all our dogs actually stick to the bed given to them by their self selected “owner” (yes, even dogs have favorites! Lol!), so luckily we’ll be able to give you a 1 Dog user review.

***We purchased a large because Tank even at 120-130 (summer weight vs winter weight - a lot more excersise in summer) is only 24” at the shoulder. I think his chest is actually as wide as he is tall at the shoulder!
**Looking at it the actual sleeping area inside the pillow seems just big enough for him because he likes to ball up when he sleeps, and to sit on it while he watches us/ while we’re still moving around the room.
**IF he was a long legged dog, or longer in the body like say a English Mastiff or a Irish Wolfhound then the larger size - a Jumbo (or XL) I believe it is- would be appropriate, but he has that bully dog blocky build, so the Large is his size.

***We will be at this point recommend this bed to friends as a middle of the road price, with unfortunately an inferior covering that requires a blanket/ additional covering,
** BUT has a as good amount of support for our dogs heavy body that is on par with dog beds/dog couches that cost nearly twice as much.
**So not a absolutely recommend dog bed, but we haven’t found one that would actually come with a absolute best dog bed ever recommendation.
** IF this manufacturer were to revise their beds with a tough covering then this would be the one we recommend to others when they visit our hobby farm, & family. **Considering the quality of their beds inner construction it is truly a shame they didn’t go all the way - then again Tank really does love a good blanket! Lol! And if you don’t like the blanket I chose (which really is a fantastic blanket) Amazon & Wally World do have a lot of throw blankets, and twin size blankets that go on sale all the time that in combination with this bed still doesn’t cost as much as the 10 year dog beds that are not nearly as soft, or supportive -as in keeping his bones off the floor- a bed as this one. (All dog beds have something it seems).

******We DO Recommend this bed - as long as you are aware the cover has it’s problems.
&
**We sincerely hope our review is helpful to you! :)

*****P.S. our pics are of how thin it is right out of the box before expansion.

top_screaming.text: SORRY FOR THE LONG REVIEW, BUT PLEASE TAKE THE TIME TO READ; IT'S SUPER IMPORTANT. I'VE USED OKO CAT LITTER "SOFT" THREE TIMES AND THEIR "ORIGINAL" ONCE. FIRST TIME USING THE SOFT, IT WAS WONDERFUL- GOOD CLUMPING, NOT VERY DUSTY, LOOKED LIKE PRODUCT PICTURE (DID TRACK, THO). THE SECOND SOFT BOX WAS SAME AS FIRST, EXCEPT CLUMPS FELL APART AND IT TOOK SEVERAL MINUTES TO SCOOP OUT ALL THE LITTLE BITS LEFT OVER. BUT THIS THIRD BOX OF SOFT LITTER... I'M LITERALLY SHOCKED! THE COMPANY CHANGED THE TYPES OF TREES THEY'RE USING TO MAKE IT. APARENTLY THE TREES COME FROM A DIFFENT LOCATION AS WELL. NOW THE "SOFT" OKO LITTER HAS HALF INCH POINTY PIECES OF WOOD THAT LEFT REAL SPLINTERS IN MY SKIN WHEN I PUT MY HAND FLAT ON TOP OF IT IN THE LITTER BOX. IT LOOKS NOTHING LIKE THEIR PREVIOUS SOFT LITTER AND PICTURE ON THE BOX. THE DUST WAS UNBEILIEVABLE WHEN I POURED IT. I COUGHED AND COUGHED AND HAD TO OPEN MANY WINDOWS BECAUSE IT SPREAD SO MUCH THROUGHOUT THE HOUSE. THIRTY MINUTES LATER, I STILL FEEL PAIN AND TIGHTNESS IN MY CHEST. I ALSO HAVE SOME RISIDUAL DUST IN MY MOUTH. IT'S BEEN SITTING, UNTOUCHED, AND ALTHOUGH MY CAT IS 30 FEET FROM THE LITTER BOX, HER EYES ARE WATERING AND SHE LOOKS LIKE SHE'S HAVING SOME TROUBLE BREATHING. CATS ARE VERY SUSEPTABLE TO UPPER RESPIRATORY INFECTONS. I DUG AROUND IN THE LITTER A BIT TO SEE HOW MUCH DUST WOULD KICK UP IN MY CAT'S FACE WHEN SHE DIGS. A TON!!! USING THIS LITTER WILL SURELY HURT HER LUNGS. THE PRICE OF ALL OKO LITTERS HAS MARKEDLY INCREASED. CLEARLY THE OKO COMPANY IS MORE INTERESTED IN PROFITS, THAN THE FACT THAT THEY HAVE STARTED MAKING A PRODUCT THAT CAN INJURE CATS. A COUPLE DAYS AGO I GOT THEIR "ORIGINAL" LITTER AND QUICKLY ORDERED THIS THIRD BOX OF SOFT AS A REPLACEMENT (ASSUMING I WOULD RECEIVE THE SAME SOFT THEY SHIPPED THE FIRST TWO TIMES), BECAUSE THE "ORIGINAL" WAS SOOO SHARP AND JAGGED- LIKE LITTLE BROKEN UP PIECES OF HARD, DRY WOOD- EVEN MORE DANGEROUS THAN THIS NEW SOFT. NONE OF WHAT I'M SAYING IS EXAGGERATED. I SEE OTHER RECENT REVIEWS STATING THE SAME THINGS ABOUT THE NEW LITTER. RESPONSES FROM THE OKO COMPANY STATE THAT BECAUSE THEY ARE NOW USING SEVERAL TYPES OF TREE WOOD, THE LITTER WILL VARY. THE COMPANY DOES NOT RESPOND TO CONCERNS THAT THE PRODUCT HAS BECOME UNSAFE, OTHER THAN TO SAY THAT THE CUSTOMER MUST HAVE RECIEVED AN OFF BATCH. HOW IRRESPONSIBLE! I'M WORRIED ABOUT MY KITTY HAVING TO GO POTTY IN THIS; IT'S THE ONLY LITTER WE HAVE TONIGHT. AN AUTOIMMUNE DISEASE MAKES IT HARD FOR ME TO LEAVE THE HOUSE VERY OFTEN, BUT NO MATTER MY SYMPTOMS, I WILL GO TO THE STORE FOR ANOTHER LITTER TOMORROW.  THIS SUCKS!

WARNING TO DOG OWNERS!
THESE FILTERS LEAK MINUTE AMOUNTS OF CHARCOAL, WHICH ARE DIFFICULT TO DETECT IN A STAINLESS STEEL WATERING PAN. I USE ONLY SPRING WATER FOR MY DOG. ABOUT THREE MONTHS AFTER I PURCHASED THESE FILTERS, I BEGAN TO NOTICE DARK, PATCHY SORES ON THE BACK OF MY 4 YEAR-OLD SHIH TZU MIX. THESE SORES WERE NOT NOTICEABLE UNDER HIS THICK COAT UNTIL THEY HAD FORMED HORRIBLE, DARK SCABS. I STARTED KEEPING HIS HAIR CUT DOWN VERY SHORT ANDI STARTED TREATING HIM WITH ANTIBIOTIC SPAY,ANTIBIOTIC OINTMENT, AND MEDICATED PRESCRIPTION SHAMPOO. MEDICATING THE SORES STILL TOOK ABOUT A WEEK BEFORE I COULD SEE SIGNIFICANT HEALING. WHILE THE METHODS I USED TO TREAT THE SORES WERE EFFECTIVE, NOTHING I DID PREVENTED THE SORES FROM REAPPEARING, SO I TOOK HIM TO THE VET. SEVERAL MONTHS OF PRESCRIPTION MEDICATION AND INJECTIONS, AS THE VET KEPT TELLING ME THESE SORES WERE CASUED BY SOME TYPE OF ALLERGY AND THE SOURCE COULD BE ANY ONE OF A NUMBER OF THINGS, NO IMPROVEMENT WAS VISIBLE. THIS WENT ON FOR NEARLY A YEAR. AFTER A BLOOD TEST REVEALED NOTHING ABNORMAL WITH MY DOG'S HEALTH, I DECIDED TO GO THROUGH A PROCESS OF ILLIMINATION WHEREBY I STOPPED GIVING HIM ALL TREATS, ALL HEALTH AIDES FOR HIS JOINTS, AND ALL DIETARY SUPPLEMENTS. AFTER 2 WEEKS, THE SORE SEEMED TO BE PROLIFERATING, SO WE CHANGED THE BLUE HEALTHY WEIGHT DOG FOOD TO A PUPPY FOOD. THE SORES CONTINUED TO BREAK OUT, SO I KNEW IT WAS NOT SOMETHING HE WAS EATING. MY DOG ONLY GOES AS FAR AS MY FRONT PORCH, SO HE DOES NOT HAVE CONTACT WITH GRASS OR WEEDS. AFTER SOME SERIOUS ANXIETY, AND OVER A THOUSAND DOLLARS OF VET BILLS, I DECIDED TO TAKE A LOOK AT THE WATERING SYSTEM AND NOTICED WHAT APPEARED TO BE TINY SPECS OF CHARCOAL IN THE WATERING PAN. I RAN THE WATER INTO A COFFEE FILTER AND THE SPECS WERE VERY VISIBLE. I CHANGED BACK TO THE PREVIOIS WATERING SYSTEM I WAS USING,WHICH DOES NOT USE A CHARCOAL FILTER, AS IT WAS THE ONLY THING I HAD NOT YET TRIED. I ONLY BOUGHT THE NEWER ONE BECAUSE I DIDN'T HAVE TO REFILL IT AS OFTEN. WITHIN TWO WEEKS, I NOTICED MY DOG WAS NOT BREAKING OUT AS BADLY, BUT I WAS STILL FINDING SORES THAT WERE ALREADY OUT FOR SOME TIME. WITHIN 4 MORE WEEKS, I NOTICED THERE WERE NO NEW SORES COMING OUT AT ALL, AND THE OLDER SORES WERE HEALING QUICKER.. GRADUALLY, I BEGAN RESTORING HIS DIETARY AIDES AND EVEN RETURNED TO THE HEALTHY WEIGHT DOG FOOD, AND TOOK HIM OFF OF THE APOQUEL. THERE WERE NO NEW BREAKOUTS AND THE SORES WERE ALMOST COMPLETELY HEALED. AFTER CONSULTING THE VET, HE TOLD ME I HAD PROBABLY FOUND THE CULPRIT RESPONSIBLE FOR HIS TERRBILE BREAKOUTS.
I STRONGLY RECOMMEND THAT NO ONE USE THIS CHARCOAL FILTER. I CONTINUE TO USE THE WATERING SYSTEM, BUT WITHOUT THE CHARCOAL FILTER AND HAVE SEEN NO BREAKOUTS ON MY DOG. I HAVE INCLUDED PHOTOS I SENT TO MY PET INSURANCE.

I BOUGHT A BARK COLLAR 7 YEARS AGO FROM THIS COMPANY WHEN MY BABY WAS A PUPPY. IT WAS AND IS WORTH ITS WEIGHT IN GOLD SO I IGNORED THE BAD REVIEWS. MY BAD.

THE BARK COLLAR IS REFILLED WITH A CANISTER OF CITRONELLA WHICH IS INEXPENSIVE. I ONLY HAD TO PUT IT ON HIM 3-4 TIMES. HE HATED IT SO MUCH, AFTER THAT, I ONLY HAD TO HOLD IT UP FOR HIM TO SEE AND HE TOOK OFF FOR THE NEAREST SAFE HAVEN; UNDER A TABLE, BED, WHATNOT.

HERE'S WHY IT'S STILL WORTH ITS WEIGHT IN GOLD. I HAVE A LITTLE 19 LB PEKAPOO (PEKINGESE AND POODLE). ANYONE WHO OWNS A SMALL DOG KNOWS (OR SHOULD) THAT IF THEY GET HOLD OF A BONE IT'S A DISASTER. 1 - YOU AREN'T GOING TO GET IT AWAY FROM THEM WITHOUT HURTING THEM IN SOME WAY (DON'T HAVE THE HEART FOR THAT) AND 2- IF YOU DON'T GET IT AWAY FROM THEM THERE'S A DEFINITE CHANCE IT COULD KILL THEM.

MY LITTLE GUY GOT A NASTY RAGGED BONE THAT WAS FORTUNATELY LONG ENOUGH FOR ME TO GRAB ONE END OF IT. I YELLED, I SWEET TALKED, I PRETENDED TO CRY. NO LUCK. SO I THOUGHT, OK, I'LL KEEP HOLD OF THE BONE WITH ONE HAND AND PICK HIM UP, CARRY HIM HOME AND FIGURE SOMETHING OUT. IN ORDER TO DO THAT, I HAD TO PUT THE LEASH HANDLE IN MY COAST POCKET TO FREE UP A HAND.. BUT THE BARK COLLAR WAS IN MY POCKET. SO I TOOK IT OUT AND WHEN HE SAW IT, HE DROPPED THE BONE!!!!!!!!!!!!

BACK TO THE BUSINESS AT HAND. THIS DEVICE IS COMPLICATED; YOU HAVE TO MEMORIZE HOW TO USE THE DIFFERENT FUNCTIONS (EITHER THAT OR CARRY CRIB NOTES AROUND). THE VIBRATING AND BEEPING FEATURES ARE COMPLETELY USELESS. I CAN BARELY FEEL THE VIBRATION AND BARELY HEAR THE BEEPING. NO DOG THAT IS GOING AFTER SOMETHING IS GOING TO PAY ANY ATTENTION. ESPECIALLY BECAUSE I'M PRETTY SURE HE CAN'T FEEL OR HEAR IT. SO THAT LEAVES THE CITRONELLA SPRAY FEATURE. NOT NEARLY AS STRONG AS THE BARK COLLAR. HE NEVER FLINCHED WHEN I ACTIVATED IT. I'S SOMEWHAT HEAVY SO IT MOVES A LITTLE WHEN HE RUNS SO IT MUST HAVE MISSED HIM BECAUSE AS WE KNOW FROM THE ABOVE STORY - WE KNOW HOW MUCH HE HATES THAT CITRONELLA!

IT'S NOT A RETURNABLE ITEM SO I JUST CONTACTED AMAZON DIRECTLY AND VOILA, THREW IT AWAY AND GOT MY MONEY BACK.

I READ THE VENDOR'S RESPONSES TO THE NEGATIVE COMMENTS AND I REALLY APPRECIATE IT WHEN A COMPANY TRIES TO MAKE THINGS RIGHT WHICH WAS ANOTHER REASON I TOOK A CHANCE. BUT I DON'T HAVE THE TIME NOR THE PATIENCE FOR DIFFICULT TO DEAL WITH PRODUCTS ESPECIALLY WHEN THEY DON'T WORK. SOMEBODY NEEDS TO GO BACK TO THE DRAWING BOARD.

I considered giving this a 4 star review but I think 5 starts is what it deserves and here is why! I have an Alaskan malamute who drooled so much in my last car, a Subaru Impreza, to the point where I had to get the motors for the windows replaced and dropping much more money than I had wanted to. After buying a new Subaru Crosstrek I knew I needed to protect my cars interior. I stumbled onto this cover that I'm enjoying after having it for the past few months now. It is durable great quality material even after washing it and does what it is intended for, protecting against excessive drooling!

I browsed at similar products and found the inserts that go into the window to be better than the others which look to be like a plastic film that looks useless. This cover has hard plastic inserts that will fit down into the door, just got to find the right spots. NOW I will mention the inserts will pop up if you roll the window all the way down and then up again, a quick fix. They will fling out of the door if the windows are all the way down and going 60-70mph+ and to be honest if it weren't for the suction cup that makes sure your cover doesn't fly out the window completely, it would of flew right out the window onto the road. I kept seeing peoples reviews about how crappy the suction cup is but a handful of times I caught it still holding on while the inserts were out of the door. THANK GOODNESS. One more thing to note, the velcro strips that come with this cover...ABSOLUTE trash. haha so I went to the store to buy the scotch brand of velcro strips($7) that work so much much better. I highly recommend you replace those crappy ones you get with your cover with the better strips to secure your cover down so it isn't flapping all over the place and doesn't just melt off in the summer time. No need to thank me! I don't consider the velcro to be apart of the cover itself so it doesn't reflect on its 5 star review I'm taking the time to write down. I do hope this helps other people come to the decision!

As luck would have it, a chihuahua set on fire by hell itself lives next door to me. Everyday without fail the little bastard runs out into its yard and barks, and barks, and barks. I couldn’t sleep, and when I did all I dreamed about was sending that dog into orbit.

Then I found this unassuming little bird house. Decided for $30 it was worth a shot and grabbed one. Lo and behold 2 days later when it arrived, that little shit was outside barking again. I woke up, walked as close to my fence as possible, and went through the power settings on the bird house. The screeching dog continued until suddenly I hit setting 2 on the device. The last thing I heard was a pulsing hum from the little bird house, and then the sound of a dog door flapping. It was finished.

This Chinese sonic weapon has given me my sleep back. I really can’t recommend it enough based on my subjective experience. I would recommend it in a heartbeat because if nothing else it’s worth a shot.

top_five_star_obscene.text:
At 14 years of age my cat has pretty much given up using the litter box. It's not because he's ill (I've taken him to the vet), his age (he's been ruining my carpets going on 10 years now), or because the litter box is too dirty, it's because he's simply just a jerk. I've lost more deposits, and spent more money on the bastard than I'd like to acknowledge. Needless to say I've tried pretty much every carpet cleaner I can find, and purchased a carpet shampooer. I was desperate to rid my house of the cat pee smell without having to re-home my cat. I would never do that. I love my carpet-destroying pain in the ass.
I purchased the carpet cleaner, gallon stain remover and the spray bottle after seeing an ad for it somewhere. I certainly didn't have high expectations, even after reading the positive reviews, but I was willing to give it a try. I pretreated the cat vomit stains with the spray bottle, doused my carpet in the Enzyme Cleaner-Pet Odor Eliminator, let it sit, used my carpet shampooer with Bubba's Carpet Cleaner, and then re-doused my carpet in the Odor Eliminator and let it sit over night. I then used my carpet shampooer with the same carpet cleaner again. My efforts might have been over kill, but I'll be damned if it didn't work, and it did! No more stains, no more cat pee odor! I'm thrilled with this stuff, and will definitely be a repeat purchaser (monthly) since the bastard squatted in the corner less than 24 hours after I last shampooed the carpet.
top_five_star_obscene.title: At 14 years of age my cat has pretty much given up using the litter box

top_five_star_obscene.text:
My cats love this thing, so far. One of my monsters is going to be 19 y/o, and he had been recently diagnosed with kidney disease. I bought this to help him and the other monsters drink more water to help with better kidney function, on top of special kidney food. At first, they were all curious and a little hesitant to drink out of it first ("what the hell is this bubbling thing, ma?"), so they sniffed at it until they were comfortable to drink out of it. Took them maybe a couple of hours til they realized this thing wasn't a big deal.

Now, this thing is fairly quiet WHEN THE WATER IS FILLED TO A LITTLE MORE THAN HALFWAY. You can't hear the motor at all. You do hear little soothing trickles, like soft rain or waterfall, if you're into that. When the water isn't full, then is sounds like a bubbly shart (it's the best way I can describe it). That's to let you know that it's refill time.

Since I have 3 monsters, I have to refill the water like every 2 days, which isn't bad compared to before where I would have to change out their water at least once a day. The filters in this fountain does help keep crap out of their water (I already give them filtered water, so their water is now double filtered. Must now taste like crisp, fresh water.) It's also easy to clean (no tubes or anything like that compared to other pet drinking fountains that I had done research on).

I'll see how the motor holds up in the next few months. I've read reviews on some people getting the crappy lot of motors, and the company seemed okay with replacing stuff? Or maybe I read that incorrectly. Anyway, for the price, it's a pretty good water fountain for your fur babies.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Very Nice!

I've owned this thing for 3 years, and it has been in constant use during that time, with 2 cats. The unit itself is very reasonably priced. As far as its value to me, I'd pay $1000.00 for this unit if I knew the grief it would save me over the years.

The spendy part comes in with the purchase of the replacement litterboxes. Although it's no small monthly fee, again, totally worth it, for many reasons.

- Don't have to worry about a nasty caked up litter box. I throw the litter box out every week and replace it with a new, fresh, clean one. The bad smells, nastiness, all the "bad stuff" go straight to the garbage, where they belong. I used to, periodically, have to take the stupid litter box outside, spray it down, clean it out, stuff spraying all over the place, let it dry, go fill it up with litter, etc,. What a PITA! Whose got time for that!

-Don't have to worry about scooping ANOTHER ANIMAL'S CRAP and disposing of it. I mean, I wouldn't do that for my own children. This is the problem with cats/ dogs in general, THEY POOP. Anyway. Now, once a week, I (or kid) take the tray, throw it in a big trash bag, and it goes in the trash. Done. Easy.

-No fighting with kids etc about CLEANING OUT THE FREAKING LITTER BOX. This was a CONSTANT battle in the past. A condition of us having a pet is that they will take care of the litter, period. I would go upstairs and look at that thing and NASTINESS PREVAILED. Now, it's easy to manage because every Tuesday, "Did you take the litter box out?" to which 8 out of 10 times is a "no." "Go do it." Much easier to supervise / manage.

I am quite please with the durability of the unit overall. The rake is powered by a low speed, high torque motor that puts the stuff in the "collector." The motor works great and I've had zero issues with the performance of this thing. The design is elegant, simple, effective.

The only thing I would change is an easier way to remove the rake and clean it. that's it. That's the only thing I'd change.

Love this thing and cannot imagine living without it.
top_five_star_obscene.title: 3 year owner, two cats, spendy but worth every penny

top_five_star_obscene.text:
Few products have as much controversy around them as this stuff. So I tried it, and I can tell you this product WORKS. Our dog has fleas for the FIRST TIME IN HIS LIFE and my wife is FREAKING OUT. We used the shampoo first, and fleas were being killed right and left. Unfortunately, the fleas came back (argh) so we are using a different approach… daily combings and local extermination. We bought the spray for spot treatment, and here's what we do:

1. Buy a flea comb.
2. Comb his or her coat going slowly and methodically using a flashlight to see inspect what the comb finds while it is still on his / her coat.
3. Fleas will either be on the top or in the middle of the comb. You don't have long before they will jump off or run away, so SPRAY IMMEDIATELY ONTO THE COMB AND COAT THE FLEA. Each time you finish a pass, check the "jail cell" of the comb (between the two rows) and see if there are any fleas trapped in there. They re less likely to get out and we just spray the entire comb, wait 30 seconds, then clear out the cadaver with a thin piece of cardboard.
4. Again… you need to act fast, and once the flea is coated, he's doomed. But they are really clever little buggers who will simply leap to freedom if you don't act fast, so we do this with two people… one combing, while the other holds the flashlight in one hand and the spray in the other.

I called the company and they told me what is in the product. Apparently there are two food grade compounds. Both are found in over the counter drugs that are used as laxatives and something else which is an acid compound… I think as a stool softener? I can't remember. But apparently you can drink a cup of this stuff and you'll have stomach cramps and really really bad diarrhea. That's safe enough for me to feel comfortable using it on my dog's coat, and our dog is FAMILY.

Basically, the first compound soaks the flea and causes his or her incredibly hard shell to soften. Damn things have armor!!!! Then the second compound, the acid, basically fries the little bastards, which, to be completely honest, thrills me no end. Call me sick, but there is nothing more enjoyable to see these nasty blood sucking buggers take a bath in this stuff and die. They are eating my dog and sucking its blood, and I owe them no mercy. Hopefully I won't go to hell for that, but I really despise these things, and if you've ever been infested, I know you agree. Victory!!!!

Here are the caveats, and I wish you cat owners out there would learn how to read. This stuff has an acid in it that WILL DRY OUT THE COAT if used more than once every three days. So don't spray the entire coat on a regular basis… no more often than once every three days. AND… cats LICK their coats, and since the compound causes uncontrollable laxative effects that could KILL YOUR CAT you must NOT use this on cats. Read the damn label people. Not for cats. Feline NIX!. Nicht fur den Meow meow. Its NOT for use on cats, and DON'T let your dogs drink or lick this stuff either. You shouldn't drink it either unless you like going to the bathroom and are looking for a terrible excuse to miss work. Its a strong laxative. Don't drink it. Not for kitties. Okay?

What I haven't figured out is how to get the fleas out of our carpet. I've tried the little flea traps with the night light. Those work too! But how long do we have to have those things in our bedroom? I'm trying DE next… I'll review the product when I get it.
